name: Arsenal FC
bio: Arsenal FC is one of England's most successful football clubs, based in North London. Known as 'The Gunners', they are famous for their attractive style of play and have a rich history spanning over 135 years.
extra_info:
— From Arsenal FC —
Arsenal has won 13 First Division and Premier League titles, 14 FA Cups, and 2 League Cups. They were the first London club to win the First Division title and went unbeaten for an entire Premier League season in 2003-04, earning the nickname 'The Invincibles'. The club moved from Highbury to the Emirates Stadium in 2006 and is owned by Kroenke Sports & Entertainment.

— From https://www.transfermarkt.co.uk/arsenal-fc/ —
Nickname: The Gunners; Colors: Red and white; Ground opened: 2006; Record transfer fee paid: £72m for Nicolas Pépé

name: Brighton & Hove Albion
bio: Brighton & Hove Albion Football Club, founded in 1901 and known as "The Seagulls," is a professional football club based on England's south coast that has experienced a remarkable transformation from lower-league struggles to Premier League establishment. The club's greatest historical achievement came in 1983 when they reached the FA Cup Final, though they endured decades of financial difficulties and ground-sharing arrangements before moving to their modern Amex Stadium in 2011. Under the ownership of Tony Bloom and strategic leadership, Brighton has cultivated a progressive, data-driven culture focused on developing young talent and playing attractive football, earning promotion to the Premier League in 2017 for the first time in their history. The Seagulls have successfully established themselves as a stable Premier League club, consistently finishing in mid-table positions while building a reputation for astute recruitment and tactical innovation under various managers.

name: Crystal Palace
bio:
Crystal Palace Football Club is an English professional football club based in South Norwood, London. They currently play in the highest level in English football, the Premier League. Since 1964, the club have mostly played in the top two leagues of English football. The club was founded in 1905 at the site of the famous Crystal Palace Exhibition building by the owners of the FA Cup Final stadium, who wanted their own team to play at the historic venue. Palace applied to be elected to The Football League, but this was rejected and they instead joined the Southern Football League Second Division, playing home games at The Crystal Palace, inspiration for the club's initial nickname, "The Glaziers". Palace won the Division and promotion in their first season, and played in the Southern League First Division for the next fifteen years.

In 1920 the Southern League Division One formed the Football League Third Division. Palace won the division and gained promotion to the Second Division, where they spent four seasons before suffering relegation to the Third Division South. In 1958 a league re-organisation saw Palace become founder members of Division Four. Over the next eleven years the club moved from the lowest rung of English Football to the highest, reaching the First Division in 1969, and staying in the top division for four seasons before suffering successive relegations. In 1973 the club modernised its image, changing the nickname from The Glaziers to "The Eagles" and ending the 68-year association with claret and blue by introducing the red-and-blue vertical stripes now associated with the club. The club stabilised itself in the top two divisions with promotions in 1977 and 1979, the latter saw the club crowned as Division Two Champions. The period between 1989–91 was a successful time for the club. They reached an FA Cup Final in 1990, won the Full Members Cup in 1991, and achieved third place in the top division in the 1990–1991 season. Palace became founder members of the Premier League in 1992, but were relegated the same season, despite achieving 49 points which is still a Premier League record for a relegated club. The club then achieved promotion back to the Premier League three times in 1994, 1997 and 2004, but each time suffered relegation at the end of the following season. Palace entered administration in both 2000 and 2010, and are now owned by a consortium of four. The club were promoted back to the Premier League with a 1–0 win over Watford in the Football League play-offs in May 2013.

Crystal Palace initially played their games in the grounds of The Crystal Palace, but the First World War saw them forced to move out, and they enjoyed a number of seasons at both the Herne Hill Velodrome and The Nest. Since 1924, Palace have played their home games at Selhurst Park. Their home colours are red and blue vertical stripes, though prior to 1973 they wore claret and pale blue after the fashion of Aston Villa. They have a fierce rivalry with M23 neighbours Brighton & Hove Albion, with whom they have contested the M23 derby 98 times and also enjoy a strong rivalry with fellow South London team Millwall. The club's kit is currently made by Macron, and the shirt sponsor is Neteller. The club captain is Mile Jedinak and the current player of the year is Julian Speroni. Edmund Goodman is the club's longest serving manager, and Jim Cannon has made the most appearances for the club. Peter Simpson is the club's top scorer for both one season and overall, netting 54 and 165 respectively. The highest transfer fee received has been for Wilfried Zaha from Manchester United in January 2013.

name: Leicester City
bio:
Leicester City Football Club, founded in 1884, is an English professional football club based in Leicester that currently competes in the EFL Championship following relegation from the Premier League in 2023. The Foxes achieved one of the greatest underdog stories in sports history by winning the Premier League title in the 2015-16 season against 5000-1 odds, and have also claimed the FA Cup (2021) and Football League Championship three times throughout their history. 

Known for their passionate fanbase at the King Power Stadium and their blue home kit, Leicester City has built a reputation for developing young talent and playing attractive, counter-attacking football under various managers. Despite their recent relegation, the club remains determined to return to the Premier League and continue building on their remarkable legacy as a team that defied expectations on football's biggest stage.

name: Manchester City
bio:
Manchester City Football Club, founded in 1880 and based in Manchester, England, has transformed from a historically modest club into one of the world's most successful football powerhouses. 

Under the ownership of the City Football Group since 2008, the club has won multiple Premier League titles, FA Cups, and achieved their first UEFA Champions League victory in 2023, establishing themselves as a dominant force in both domestic and European competition. Known for their attacking, possession-based style of play under manager Pep Guardiola and their passionate fanbase, City has cultivated a culture of excellence while maintaining strong community ties in Manchester. 

Currently competing at the highest level of English and European football, Manchester City continues to set new standards for success in the modern game.

name: Nottingham Forest
bio:
Nottingham Forest Football Club is a professional football club based in West Bridgford, Nottinghamshire, England. The club competes in the Premier League, the top tier of English football.  The club, often referred to simply as Forest or Nottm Forest have played home matches at the City Ground since 1898.

Founded in 1865, Forest were founder members of the Football Alliance in 1889 and joined the Football League in 1892. Forest won the FA Cup in 1898 and 1959. Their most successful period was under the management of Brian Clough between 1975 and 1993, winning the League, back to back European Cups, four League Cups and two Full Members Cups.

name: Stoke City
bio:
Stoke City Football Club, founded in 1863 and based in Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, is one of England's oldest professional football clubs and was a founding member of the Football League in 1888. 

The Potters achieved their greatest success during their decade-long Premier League tenure (2008-2018), reaching the FA Cup Final in 2011 and qualifying for European competition, while becoming renowned for their passionate fanbase and the intimidating atmosphere at the bet365 Stadium. Known for their working-class roots and strong community ties reflecting the industrial heritage of the Potteries region, 

Stoke developed a distinctive direct style of play under Tony Pulis that made them a formidable opponent for top-flight teams. Following relegation to the Championship in 2018, the club has been working to rebuild and secure promotion back to the Premier League while maintaining their identity as a proud, no-nonsense footballing institution.

name: Unione Sportiva Cremonese
bio:
Unione Sportiva Cremonese, founded in 1903 in the historic city of Cremona, is one of Italy's oldest football clubs, known for their distinctive red and grey striped jerseys and passionate local fanbase. The club has primarily competed in Serie B and lower divisions throughout their history, with their greatest achievement being their recent promotion to Serie A in 2022, marking their return to Italy's top flight after 26 years. 

Cremonese is deeply rooted in their community and represents the proud footballing tradition of Lombardy, playing their home matches at the intimate Stadio Giovanni Zini. Currently competing in Serie A, the club faces the challenge of establishing themselves among Italy's elite while maintaining their strong regional identity and developing young talent through their respected youth academy system.
extra_info: Cremonese finished 4th in Serie B during the 2024-25 season and secured promotion to Serie A through the play-offs. The club is owned by Giovanni Arvedi and is known by several nicknames including 'The Violins', reflecting Cremona's famous tradition of violin making.

name: Blackpool
bio:
Blackpool Football Club is a professional association football club based in the seaside resort of Blackpool, Lancashire, England. The team competes in EFL League One, the third tier of the English football league system. 

Founded in 1887, the club played home games at Raikes Hall and the Athletic Grounds before moving to Bloomfield Road in 1901.

The club were founder members of the Lancashire League in 1889 and were invited into the Football League Second Division in 1896. They failed re-election in 1899 but had their Football League membership reinstated the following year. Blackpool remained in the Second Division until they won the league title in 1929–30, though they were relegated after three seasons in the First Division. Promoted again in 1936–37, the club entered a golden period under the stewardship of long-time manager Joe Smith. Blackpool lost the 1948 and 1951 FA Cup finals before winning the competition in 1953, in the so-called "Matthews Final", in which they beat Bolton Wanderers 4–3, overturning a 3–1 deficit in the closing stages of the game. That same year, four Blackpool players were in the England team which lost against Hungary at Wembley. In the 1950s they had four top-six finishes in the First Division, with their best position being runners-up to Manchester United in 1955–56. In 1956, Stanley Matthews was the inaugural recipient of the Ballon d'Or.

Blackpool were relegated out of the First Division in 1967 and again in 1971 after winning promotion in 1969–70. They dropped into the fourth tier after suffering relegations in 1978 and 1981, then gained promotion in 1984–85. Between 1987 and 2019, the club was owned by the Oyston family. Nine years after buying the club, Owen Oyston was jailed for the 1992 rape and indecent assault of a 16-year-old girl. His wife, Vicki, took over the chairmanship of the club during her husband's three-year prison term. The couple's son, Karl, took over in 1999 and remained in the role for 19 years. In 2018, after the Oyston family was found, in a High Court judgement, to have operated an "illegitimate stripping" of the club, paying out £26.77 million to companies they owned, Owen relieved Karl of his role and gave it to his daughter, Natalie. In June 2019, Simon Sadler, a 49-year-old Blackpool-born businessman, bought a 96.2% share in the club, completely removing the Oyston family from any involvement.

During the long years of Oyston ownership, Blackpool were relegated into the fourth tier in 1990, promoted via the play-offs in 1992, then relegated again in 2000. Blackpool then gradually reached the Premier League after becoming the first club to gain promotion from every division of the Football League via the play-off system; they won the 2001 Third Division play-offs, 2007 League One play-offs and the 2010 Championship play-offs. They spent one season in the Premier League, under manager Ian Holloway, and later suffered a double relegation into League Two by 2016, though gained immediate promotion by winning the 2017 League Two play-off final.

name: Barnsley
bio:
Barnsley Football Club is a professional football club in Barnsley, South Yorkshire, England. The club competes in EFL League One, the third tier of English football. 
Barnsley Football Club nicknamed the Tykes, they were founded in 1887 by Reverend Tiverton Preedy under the name Barnsley St. Peter's. The club colours are red and white, and their home ground since 1888 has been Oakwell.

name: Stockport County
bio:
Stockport County Football Club is a professional football club based in Edgeley, Stockport, England, which competes in EFL League One, the third tier of the English football league system.  Formed in 1883 as Heaton Norris Rovers, the team adopted their name in 1890 after the County Borough of Stockport. They have played at Edgeley Park since 1902, traditionally in blue and white, and are nicknamed The Hatters after the town's former hat-making industry.

Stockport County joined the Football League in 1900 and competed in it continuously from 1905 to 2011. The team spent most of their history in the lower reaches of the Football League, but the 1990s were more successful with the club competing in the First Division (now known as the Championship) for five seasons, whilst they also reached the League Cup semi-finals in 1996–97. However, instability on and off the pitch eventually led to Stockport falling back to the lower divisions. The club started the 2011–12 season in the Conference National, having been relegated from Football League Two for the first time in their history at the end of 2010–11. At the end of 2012–13, Stockport were relegated to the Conference North.

name: Burnley
bio: Burnley Football Club, founded in 1882 and based in Lancashire, England, is one of the founding members of the Football League and boasts a rich history that includes two First Division titles (1921, 1960) and an FA Cup victory in 1914. Known as "The Clarets" for their traditional claret and blue colors, the club plays at Turf Moor and has built a reputation for its passionate working-class fanbase and commitment to developing homegrown talent while maintaining strong community ties. Under manager Sean Dyche's long tenure, Burnley established itself as a resilient Premier League side known for organized, physical football and punching above their weight against bigger clubs. Currently competing in the Championship following relegation in 2022, the club continues to embody the determined spirit of its industrial town while working toward a return to England's top flight.

name: Coventry City
bio:
Coventry City Football Club, founded in 1883, is a professional soccer club based in Coventry, England, currently competing in the EFL Championship. The Sky Blues achieved their greatest triumph in 1987 by winning the FA Cup with a memorable 3-2 victory over Tottenham Hotspur at Wembley Stadium, marking the club's only major trophy to date. 

Known for their passionate fanbase and resilient spirit, Coventry has experienced significant challenges in recent years, including a controversial temporary relocation to Northampton and Birmingham due to stadium disputes. Following their return to the Coventry Building Society Arena, the club has stabilized in the Championship under manager Mark Robins and continues to build toward recapturing their former glory days. They were promoted back to the Premier League in 2025 for the first time in 23years

name: Sunderland
bio:
Sunderland Association Football Club is an English professional football club based in Sunderland that plays in the Premier League. Since its formation in 1879, the club has won six First Division titles—in 1892, 1893, 1895, 1902, 1913, and 1936, the FA Cup twice, in 1937 and 1973 and the FA Community Shield in 1936. They were also Football League Cup finalists in 1985 and 2014.

Sunderland won their first FA Cup in 1937 with a 3–1 victory over Preston North End, and remained in the top league for 68 successive seasons until they were relegated for the first time in 1958. Sunderland's most notable trophy after the Second World War was their second FA Cup in 1973, when the club secured a 1–0 victory over Leeds United. The team has won the second tier title five times in that period and the third tier title once.

Sunderland play their home games at the 48,707 capacity all-seater Stadium of Light having moved from Roker Park in 1997.The original ground capacity was 42,000 which was increased to 49,000 following expansion in 2000. Sunderland have a long-standing rivalry with their neighbouring club Newcastle United, with whom they have contested the Tyne–Wear derby since 1898. Since their establishment in the Premier League in 2007, Sunderland have became one of the world's wealthiest football brands, being valued at USD $93 million in 2014, 31st in the world overall

name: AC Milan
bio:
Associazione Calcio Milan, commonly referred to as A.C. Milan or simply Milan, is a professional football club in Milan, Italy, founded in 1899. The club has spent its entire history, with the exception of the 1980–81 and 1982–83 seasons, in the top flight of Italian football, known as Serie A since 1929–30.

With 18 officially recognised UEFA and FIFA titles, they are the fourth most successful club in the world, along with Boca Juniors, and the most successful Italian club at international level. Milan has won a joint record three Intercontinental Cups and one FIFA Club World Cup, seven European Cup/Champions League titles (Italian record), the UEFA Super Cup a joint record five times and the Cup Winners' Cup twice. With 18 league titles, Milan is the joint-second most successful club in Serie A, along with local rivals Internazionale and behind Juventus (33). They have also won the Coppa Italia five times, and the Supercoppa Italiana seven.

Milan's home games are played at San Siro, also known as the Stadio Giuseppe Meazza. The stadium, which is shared with city rivals Internazionale, is the largest in Italian football, with a total capacity of 80,018. Inter are considered their biggest rivals, and matches between the two teams are called Derby della Madonnina, which is one of the most followed derbies in football.

The former owner of the club was former Italian prime minister Silvio Berlusconi, who owned the club for 31 years. The club is one of the wealthiest in Italian and world football. It was a founding member of the now-defunct G-14 group of Europe's leading football clubs as well as its replacement, the European Club Association.

name: Hellas Verona Football Club
bio: Hellas Verona Football Club, founded in 1903 in Verona, Italy, is a historic Serie A club renowned for one of football's greatest underdog stories when they remarkably won the 1984-85 Serie A title under coach Osvaldo Bagnoli. The club's golden era also included notable European campaigns and the development of legendary players like Preben Elkjær, while their passionate fanbase at the Stadio Marcantonio Bentegodi has maintained unwavering support through decades of fluctuation between Italy's top divisions. Known for their yellow and blue colors and nicknamed "Gialloblu," Verona has built a reputation for developing young talent and playing attractive, attacking football despite often operating with modest resources. Currently competing in Serie A after their recent promotion, Hellas Verona continues to punch above their weight as a respected mid-table club that embodies the spirit and tradition of Italian football.
extra_info: Hellas Verona's Serie A triumph in 1984-85 remains one of the most surprising championship wins in Italian football, as they defeated much larger and more established clubs. The club is commonly known simply as Verona or Hellas within the city itself. They have experienced various divisions throughout their history but continue to compete at the top level of Italian football.

name: Peterborough United
bio:
Peterborough United Football Club is a professional football club in Peterborough, Cambridgeshire, England. The team compete in League One, the third tier of English football league system, but will compete in the Championship in the 2021–22 season following promotion. Peterborough have long-standing rivalries with nearby clubs Northampton Town, with whom they contest the Nene derby, and Cambridge United, with whom they contest the Cambridgeshire derby. They have spent their entire history at London Road Stadium and are nicknamed "The Posh".

Peterborough United formed in 1934 and joined the Midland League. Having won the Midland League title for five consecutive seasons from 1955–56, they were elected into the Football League in 1960. Peterborough immediately won the Fourth Division title in 1960–61, scoring a Football League record 134 goals. Relegated in 1968, they won another Fourth Division title in 1973–74, though suffered a further relegation in 1979. Peterborough were promoted back into the Third Division at the end of the 1990–91 season and reached the second tier with victory in the 1992 play-off final. However they returned to the fourth tier with relegations in 1994 and 1997.

Peterborough won the Third Division play-offs in 2000 under the stewardship of Barry Fry, though were relegated in 2005. They secured a place in the Championship after manager Darren Ferguson led them to consecutive promotions in 2007–08 and 2008–09 and spent three of the next four seasons in the second tier, winning a play-off final in 2011 after relegation the previous year. They were relegated back into League One in 2013, though went on to win the Football League Trophy in 2014.

In the 2020–21 season Peterborough were promoted back to the Championship, finishing runners-up after an eight year stay in League One, after coming back from 3–0 down to draw 3–3 against rivals Lincoln City, following a stoppage time penalty by Jonson Clarke-Harris.

name: Sheffield Wednesday
bio:
Sheffield Wednesday Football Club, founded in 1867, is one of England's oldest professional football clubs and has a rich heritage spanning over 150 years. The Owls, as they are known to their passionate fanbase, have won four First Division titles (1903, 1904, 1929, 1930) and three FA Cups (1896, 1907, 1935), establishing themselves as a significant force in English football history. 

Based at the iconic Hillsborough Stadium, the club boasts one of the most loyal and vocal supporter bases in the country, with their blue and white stripes representing a deep-rooted tradition in Sheffield's sporting culture. Currently competing in League One following relegation from the Championship, Sheffield Wednesday are working to rebuild and return to their former glory under new ownership and management.

name: Luton Town
bio:
Luton Town Football Club is a professional football club from Luton, Bedfordshire, England. The club currently competes in EFL League One, the third tier of the English football league system.
Founded in 1885, it is nicknamed "the Hatters" and affiliated to the Bedfordshire County Football Association. The club's history includes major trophy wins, several financial crises, numerous promotions and relegations, and some spells of sustained success. It was perhaps most prominent between 1982 and 1992, when it was a member of English football's top division, at that time the First Division; the team won its only major honour, the Football League Cup, in 1988.

name: Burton Albion
bio:
Burton Albion Football Club is a professional association football club in the town of Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, England. The team competes in League One, the third level of the English football league system.  The club's home ground is the Pirelli Stadium, having moved from Eton Park in 2005.

The club's nickname is The Brewers, stemming from the town's brewing heritage dating back hundreds of years.

The club competed in the non-League of English football from their founding in 1950 until 2009, despite the Football League heritage of their predecessors Burton Swifts, Burton United, and Burton Wanderers.

The town of Burton has had a chequered history of football clubs, partly due to the area's main sport traditionally in the past being rugby union. Many former Burton teams have struggled financially. Albion can claim lineage from Burton Swifts, Burton Town, Burton United and Burton Wanderers, all defunct football clubs in the town. Burton Albion were formed in 1950, and joined the Birmingham & District League

Albion began life at the Lloyds Foundry ground on Wellington Street, but high attendances meant that the club quickly searched for a more suitable home. Eton Park was built off Derby Road and officially opened on 20 September 1958, coinciding with the club's promotion to the Southern League. From then until its demolition in 2005, the Brewers played all their home games at Eton Park.

They have also had spells in the Northern Premier League (NPL), because of their location in the central Midlands. They entered the Conference in 2002 upon winning the NPL title.

Eton Park was dismantled to make way for housing, as the club had just completed a new stadium, at a cost of £7.2 million. It is directly opposite Eton Park and was named the Pirelli Stadium, situated as it was a short distance from Pirelli's factory in Burton. The stadium's capacity was originally set at 6,200, but then later raised to 6,912. The first match played there was a friendly between Burton Albion and Chester City on Saturday 16 July 2005.


Albion in action against Gillingham in the final match of the 2012–13 season
One notable result at the ground was a 0–0 draw in the FA Cup third round against Manchester United on 8 January 2006. The result of the Third Round replay, lucratively played at Old Trafford, was a 5–0 win for Manchester United in front of over 11,000 Brewers fans. This constituted a record number of away fans seen at Old Trafford. On 8 January 2011, in the FA Cup third round, Burton continued their FA success, knocking out Championship team Middlesbrough 2–1. Shaun Harrad scored twice at the Pirelli Stadium in the last 8 minutes to bring Burton back from 1–0 down.

After their FA Cup draw against Manchester United, Burton competed in the upper reaches of the Conference. The Brewers attained their highest ever league position to date in 2007–08, finishing in the end of season play-off places for the first time in their history, before losing in their play-off to Cambridge United. As of April 2009, they were top of the Conference National, setting a league record for the most consecutive wins. This is despite losing the services of manager Nigel Clough to Derby County with Roy McFarland installed as caretaker manager until the end of the season. When the team was 19 points clear at the top of the table, Conference sponsors Blue Square declared Burton the winners of the 2008/09 title in a PR stunt in which they paid out all bets. After that announcement in February, however, the club saw their lead reduced week by week, though they still secured promotion to the Football League, despite losing 2–1 away at Torquay United in the final game of the season when Cambridge United could only manage a goalless draw to Altrincham.

At the end of the season, it was decided that Roy McFarland would not stay permanently as the Burton manager. After a lengthy search, the club announced that Paul Peschisolido would become the club's new manager with Gary Rowett his assistant. Burton's first win in the Football League was a 5–2 win over Morecambe at the Pirelli Stadium.

The Brewers finished a respectable 13th in their first campaign in the Football League, and 19th in their second campaign after a season much interrupted by the weather. Following an end of season collapse, Burton fell from 5th place on Boxing Day to 17th place at the end of the 2011–12 season, which included a 17 game winless run and the sacking of Paul Peschisolido. In Gary Rowett's first full season in charge, he led them to 4th and the play-offs, missing out on automatic promotion by two points. Following the fourth place finish, Burton lost their play-off semi-final 4–5 on aggregate to Bradford City despite winning the first leg 3–2 at Valley Parade. After this season the Brewers came back and, despite finishing 6th in the 2013–14 season, they reached the play-off final but lost a narrow match 1–0 against Fleetwood Town. During the 2014–15 season Rowett left to join Birmingham City, and was replaced by Jimmy Floyd Hasselbaink.

name: Derby County
bio:
Derby County Football Club is a professional association football club in Derby, England. The club compete in the Championship, the second tier of English football. Notable for being one of the twelve founder members of the Football League in 1888, it is one of only ten clubs to have competed in every season of the English football league system and, in 2009, was ranked 137th in the top 200 European football teams of the 20th century by International Federation of Football History and Statistics.

Derby County F.C. was founded in 1884, by William Morley, as an offshoot of Derbyshire County Cricket Club; it has spent all but four seasons in the top two divisions of English football. The club's competitive peak came in the 1970s when they were twice English League Champions and competed in major European competitions on four separate occasions, reaching the European Cup semi-finals, as well as winning several minor trophies. Additionally, the club was a strong force in the interwar years and won the 1945–46 FA Cup.

The club adopted its now traditional black and white club colours in the 1890s and appropriated its club nickname The Rams, a tribute to its links with the First Regiment of Derby Militia, which took a ram as its mascot and the song "The Derby Ram" as its regimental song, at the same time. Home games are played at the iPro Stadium, located in Pride Park, Derby, where the club moved in 1997.

name: Pisa SC
bio:
Pisa Sporting Club, commonly referred to as Pisa, is an Italian football club based in Pisa, Tuscany. The team currently plays in Serie B.

The club was founded in 1909 as Pisa Sporting Club and refounded in 1994 as Pisa Calcio (and registered in Eccellenza, the regional football division in Italy), after the cancellation of the former because of economical troubles. It was excluded again from Italian football in 2009, after the property failed to collect enough money to pay off the club's debts. In summer 2009 it was refounded with the denomination A.C. Pisa 1909.

Pisa won two Mitropa Cups, in 1986 and 1988. They play their home matches at Arena Garibaldi – Stadio Romeo Anconetani, named after Romeo Anconetani, the chairman who brought and led the club in Serie A during the 1980s. In 2016, Giuseppe Corrado bought the club and planned the new Pisa stadium. In January 2021, billionaire Alexander Knaster acquired a 75% stake in the club.

name: Plymouth Argyle
bio:
Plymouth Argyle Football Club is a professional association football club based in the city of Plymouth, Devon, England. The team currently competes in EFL League One, the third level of the English football league system. The club has played at Home Park since 1901. 

The club takes its nickname, "The Pilgrims", from an English religious group that left Plymouth for the New World in 1620. The club crest features the Mayflower, the ship that carried the pilgrims to Massachusetts. The club has predominantly played in yellow and white throughout their history, with a few exceptions in the late 1960s and early 1970s when white was the colour of choice. A darker shade of green, described (by some) as 'Argyle green', was adopted in the 2001–02 season, and has been used ever since. The city of Plymouth is the largest in England never to have hosted top-flight football. They are the most southerly and westerly League club in England and the only professional club named Argyle.

Originally founded simply as Argyle in 1886, the club turned professional and entered both the Southern League and Western League as Plymouth Argyle in 1903. They won the Western League title in 1904–05 and the Southern League title in 1912–13, before winning election into the Football League Third Division in 1920. Finishing as runners-up on six consecutive occasions, they eventually won promotion as Third Division South champions under the long-serving management of Bob Jack in 1929–30. A 20-year stay in the Second Division ended in 1950, though they returned again as Third Division South champions in 1951–52. After another relegation in 1956 they again proved too strong for the third tier, winning the Third Division title not long after in 1958–59.

Argyle were relegated out of the Second Division in 1968, 1977 and 1992, having won promotion out of the Third Division as runners-up in 1974–75 and 1985–86. They were relegated into the fourth tier for the first time in 1995, and though they would win immediate promotion in 1995–96, they were relegated again in 1998. Promoted as champions under Paul Sturrock with 102 points in 2001–02, they secured a record fifth third tier league title in 2003–04, and would remain in the Championship for six seasons until administration and two successive relegations left them in League Two by 2011. In 2016–17 Argyle won promotion to League One, and again in 2019–20 following relegation the previous season.

name: Huddersfield Town
bio:
Huddersfield Town Association Football Club is a professional association football club based in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, England. They compete in EFL League One, the third tier of English football. 

In 1926, Huddersfield became the first English club to win three successive league titles, a feat which only three other clubs have matched, and none has bettered. They also won the FA Cup in 1922.

Nicknamed The Terriers, the club plays in blue and white vertically striped shirts and white shorts. They have played home games at the John Smith's Stadium since 1994. The stadium replaced Leeds Road, Huddersfield Town's home since 1908.

name: Juventus Football Club S.p.A.
bio:
Juventus Football Club S.p.A., founded in 1897 in Turin, Italy, stands as one of the most successful and prestigious clubs in world football, having won a record 36 Serie A titles and established itself as a dominant force in both domestic and European competitions. The club has claimed numerous international honors, including two UEFA Champions League titles, three UEFA Europa League trophies, and two Intercontinental Cups, while also achieving the rare feat of winning all major UEFA club competitions. 

Known for their iconic black and white striped jerseys and nicknamed "La Vecchia Signora" (The Old Lady), Juventus has cultivated a winning culture built on tactical excellence, defensive solidity, and a commitment to developing world-class talent throughout their storied history. Currently competing in Serie A following their return from a brief second-division stint in 2006-2007, Juventus remains one of Italy's most valuable and globally recognized football brands, continuing to attract elite players and compete at the highest levels of European football.
extra_info: Juventus is historically the most successful club in Italian football with fifty-five official titles including a record thirty-six league titles, thirteen Italian cups, and eleven European/international titles. In 1985, they became the first club to win all three major UEFA competitions and remains the only club to have won all possible continental competitions and the world title. According to FIFA-recognized rankings, Juventus was Italy's best club and second in Europe of the 20th century.

name: Como 1907
bio:
Como 1907, commonly referred to as Como, is an Italian football club based in Como, Lombardy, which currently playing in Serie C as of 2020–21 season. The club was founded in 1907, and the team's colour is royal blue.

Como were in Serie A in 2002–03; this was followed by three consecutive relegations that brought the team down in Serie C2 at the end of the 2004–05 season after having lost a playoff (2–1 on aggregate) to Novara Calcio, and being then even cancelled from Italian professional football because of bankruptcy. They were successively admitted to Serie D, the top level of non-professional football in Italy, where they spent three seasons before finally managing to win promotion back to Serie C2 in the 2007–08 season. After a further bankruptcy in 2016, a new company re-founded the club in 2017 and was admitted to Serie D from 2017 to 2018 season.

name: Torino Football Club S.p.A.
bio: Torino Football Club S.p.A., founded in 1906, is one of Italy's most historically significant football clubs, having won seven Serie A titles and five Coppa Italia trophies during their golden era in the 1940s. The club's legacy was tragically marked by the 1949 Superga air disaster that claimed the lives of the legendary "Grande Torino" team, widely considered one of the greatest football teams in history. Known for their passionate fanbase and deep-rooted Piedmontese identity, Torino maintains a fierce rivalry with Juventus in the Derby della Mole, one of Italy's most intense local derbies. Currently competing in Serie A after overcoming financial difficulties and relegation in the 2000s, the club continues to rebuild under the ownership of Urbano Cairo while honoring their rich history and pursuing a return to European competition.
extra_info: The club has a rich history dating back over a century and has competed in Serie A. They finished 11th in the 2024-25 Serie A season and play their home matches at the Stadio Olimpico Grande Torino with a capacity of nearly 28,000.

name: Bradford City
bio:
Bradford City Association Football Club is an English professional football club in Bradford, West Yorkshire. The club competes in EFL League One, the third tier of English football, and is managed by Graham Alexander. 

The club was founded in 1903. It was immediately elected into Division Two of the Football League despite not having played a previous game. Promotion to the top tier followed in 1908 and the club won the FA Cup in 1911, its only major honour. After relegation in 1922 from Division One, the club spent 77 years outside the top flight until promotion to the Premier League in 1999. City stayed up, with a then record low of 36 points, in the first season in the Premier League. Relegation followed the following season. Since then a series of financial crises did push the club to the brink of closure. The financial pressure resulted in two more relegations to League Two. In the 2012–13 season, they became the first ever team from the fourth tier of English football to reach a major domestic Wembley cup final, the Football League Cup, losing 5–0 to Swansea City. In the same season, they returned to Wembley for the playoff final and won promotion to League One with a 3–0 win over Northampton Town.

The club's colours are claret and amber and they play their home games at Valley Parade. The ground was the site of a fire on 11 May 1985, which took the lives of 56 supporters.

Bradford City have had more than 40 managers, all of whom have been from Great Britain or Ireland. The current manager is Stuart McCall.

The club is linked with American amateur side SC United Bantams.

name: Mansfield Town
bio:
Mansfield Town Football Club is a professional football club based in the town of Mansfield, Nottinghamshire, England. The club was formed in 1897 as Mansfield Wesleyans, changing its name to Mansfield Wesley in 1906 before settling on Mansfield Town in 1910, and are nicknamed “The Stags”. They traditionally play in a royal blue and amber kit. The club currently competes in League Two, the fourth tier of English football. Mansfield have lifted four professional trophies, winning the Fourth Division title in 1974–75, the Third Division in 1976–77, the Football League Trophy in 1986–87 and the Football Conference title in 2012–13. The Stags also finished as runners-up in the 2010–11 FA Trophy.
    Since 1919, Mansfield have played at Field Mill, which is now an all-seater stadium with a capacity of 9,186. Their main rivals are Chesterfield and Notts County.

name: Birmingham City Football Club
bio: Birmingham City Football Club, founded in 1875, is a historic English football club based in Birmingham that currently competes in the EFL Championship. The Blues' greatest achievement came in 2011 when they won the League Cup, defeating Arsenal 2-1 at Wembley Stadium, marking their most recent major trophy and securing European competition. Known for their passionate fanbase and the intense Second City Derby rivalry with Aston Villa, Birmingham City plays their home matches at St. Andrew's Stadium, which has been their home since 1906. Despite facing financial challenges and ownership changes in recent years, the club maintains its Championship status while working to rebuild stability and pursue promotion back to the Premier League.
extra_info: The club's most successful period was in the 1950s and early 1960s, reaching the 1956 FA Cup Final and winning the League Cup in 1963 and 2011. They have a fierce rivalry with Aston Villa in the Second City derby. The club famously retired Jude Bellingham's number 22 shirt after he became their record transfer and the world's most expensive 17-year-old when moving to Borussia Dortmund.

name: Chelsea
bio: Chelsea Football Club, founded in 1905 and based in West London, has evolved from a modest beginning to become one of England's most successful clubs, particularly flourishing after Roman Abramovich's 2003 takeover which ushered in an era of unprecedented investment and success. The Blues have captured numerous major trophies including six Premier League titles, eight FA Cups, and two UEFA Champions League victories (2012, 2021), establishing themselves as a dominant force in both domestic and European competition. Known for their attacking style of play, passionate fanbase, and iconic blue kit, Chelsea plays their home matches at the historic Stamford Bridge stadium, which has been their fortress for over a century. Currently competing at the highest level of English football under new ownership following Abramovich's departure, Chelsea continues to attract world-class talent and remains a formidable contender in the Premier League and European tournaments.

name: AS Roma
bio: Associazione Sportiva Roma is a professional football club based in Rome, Italy. Founded in 1927, Roma has participated in the top-tier of Italian football for all of its existence except for the 1951–52 season. Roma has won Serie A three times, in 1941–42, 1982–83 and 2000–01, as well as nine Coppa Italia titles and two Intercontinental Cups.
extra_info:
This page contains AS Roma's logo history, showing the evolution of their visual identity from 2000 to present, with logos used during 2000-2013, 2013-2016, 2016-2025, and the current logo from 2025-present.

— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AS_Roma —
Known as I Giallorossi (The Yellow and Reds) due to their traditional colors. The club was formed through a merger of three older Italian clubs. They play their home matches at the Stadio Olimpico, which they share with city rivals Lazio.

name: Genoa Cricket and Football Club SpA
bio:
Genoa Cricket and Football Club SpA, founded in 1893, holds the distinction of being Italy's oldest football club and played a pivotal role in establishing the sport in the country. The Rossoblu have captured nine Serie A titles, with their golden era occurring in the early 20th century, and have maintained a passionate fanbase despite experiencing fluctuations between Italy's top divisions throughout their history. 

Known for their fierce local rivalry with Sampdoria in the Derby della Lanterna, Genoa's culture is deeply rooted in working-class tradition and unwavering loyalty from their supporters at the iconic Stadio Luigi Ferraris. 

Currently competing in Serie A after their recent promotion, the club continues to fight for stability in Italy's top flight while honoring their rich heritage as pioneers of Italian football.
extra_info: Genoa have won Serie A nine times, with their first title coming in 1898 and their last in 1923-24. They also won the Coppa Italia once and are historically the fourth most successful Italian club in terms of championships won. The club was notably supported by legendary Italian sports journalist Gianni Brera, who created their nickname 'Vecchio Balordo' (Old Fool).

name: Exeter City
bio:
Exeter City Football Club is a professional association football club based in Exeter, Devon, England. The team plays in EFL League One, the third level of the English football league system. The club is owned by the club's supporters through the Exeter City Supporters' Trust.

The club was a member of the Football League from 1920 to 2003. Following five seasons in the Conference National, Exeter were promoted back to League Two for the 2008–09 season and immediately achieved automatic promotion to League One for the 2009–10 season. In the 2011–12 season of League One Exeter City were relegated to League Two, finishing 23rd with 48 points, they have remained in League Two ever since.

Exeter City was founded in 1904 and began playing on an old field used for fattening pigs, St James Park (not to be confused with the homes of Newcastle United or Brackley Town). Exeter remain at St James Park to this day. The club is nicknamed "The Grecians". For the 2016–17 season City's home kit is supplied by Joma and it consists of red and white shirts, black shorts, and black and white socks.

The club is known as the first side to play a national team from Brazil, after a tour of South America in 1914 to generate awareness of football in the continent. As a result, City and Brazilian side Fluminense are now also partner clubs.

name: Wycombe Wanderers
bio:
Wycombe Wanderers Football Club is a professional association football club based in the town of High Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, England. The team competes in League One, the third level of the English football league system.  They play their home matches at Adams Park, located on the western outskirts of High Wycombe, having moved after 95 years at Loakes Park in 1990. The club traditionally plays in quartered shirts of navy (Oxford blue) and pale blue (Cambridge blue). The club's nicknames are "The Chairboys" and "The Blues".

Founded in 1887, they entered the Southern League in 1896. They switched to the Great Western Suburban League in 1908 and then the Spartan League in 1919, before joining the Isthmian League after winning the Spartan League in 1919–20 and 1920–21. They spent 64 years in the Isthmian League, winning eight league titles and one FA Amateur Cup title. Having rejected numerous invitations to join the Alliance Premier (Gola) League (now National League), they finally accepted an offer in 1985 and eventually found success in the fifth tier of English football under the management of Martin O'Neill, winning promotion into the Football League as GM Vauxhall Conference champions in 1992–93. They also lifted the FA Trophy in 1991 and 1993, and won the Conference League Cup, Conference Shield (three times) and Conference Charity Shield.

Wycombe made an immediate impact in the Football League, winning promotion out of the Third Division via the play-offs in 1994. They spent a decade in the third tier and reached the FA Cup semi-finals in 2001, though were relegated three years later. They also reached the League Cup semi-finals in 2007 and then gained promotion out of League Two in 2008–09. This was the first of four successive seasons of promotions and relegations between League Two and League One, which was followed by a decline that saw the club only avoid relegation into non-league on goal difference in 2014. The club secured promotion out of League Two in 2017–18 under the stewardship of Gareth Ainsworth, who then took the club to the Championship for the first time in the club's history with victory in the 2020 League One play-off final.

name: Associazione Calcio Firenze Fiorentina SpA
bio: ACF Fiorentina, founded in 1926 in Florence, Italy, is one of Serie A's most storied clubs, known for their distinctive purple (viola) colors that have earned them the nickname "La Viola." The club achieved their greatest successes during the 1950s and 1960s, winning two Serie A titles (1956, 1969) and six Coppa Italia trophies, while also reaching four European finals including the 1957 European Cup final. Fiorentina is deeply embedded in Florentine culture and Renaissance tradition, playing at the historic Stadio Artemio Franchi and maintaining a passionate fanbase known for their unwavering loyalty through both triumphs and relegation battles. Currently competing in Serie A under the ownership of Rocco Commisso since 2019, the club continues to rebuild its reputation as a competitive force in Italian football while honoring its rich heritage and connection to the cultural capital of Tuscany.
extra_info: Fiorentina have won two Italian Championships (1955-56, 1968-69), six Coppa Italia trophies, and one Italian Super Cup. On the European stage, they won the UEFA Cup Winners' Cup in 1960-61, finished runners-up in the 1956-57 European Cup losing to Real Madrid, and were UEFA Cup runners-up in 1989-90. The club finished 6th in Serie A in the 2024-25 season.

name: Atalanta BC
bio:
Atalanta Bergamasca Calcio, founded in 1907 in Bergamo, Italy, has evolved from a modest provincial club into one of Serie A's most exciting and innovative teams. Under the guidance of coach Gian Piero Gasperini since 2016, the club has gained international recognition for their high-intensity, attacking style of play that emphasizes youth development and tactical creativity. 

The Nerazzurri have achieved remarkable success in recent years, including consecutive UEFA Champions League qualifications and a historic Europa League final appearance in 2022, while consistently finishing in European competition spots despite operating with one of the league's smaller budgets. Known for their passionate fanbase and commitment to developing young talent at their state-of-the-art training facilities, Atalanta continues to punch above their weight in Serie A, representing the heart and determination of their working-class Bergamo community.
extra_info:
The club participated in the 2019-20 Serie A season and also competed in the Coppa Italia during that campaign.

— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atalanta_BC —
Known for their attacking style of play under Gasperini, reached Champions League quarter-finals in 2019-20 and 2021-22. Plays home matches at Gewiss Stadium (formerly Stadio Atleti Azzurri d'Italia). Club colors are blue and black.

name: Villarreal Club de Fútbol S.A.D.
bio:
Villarreal Club de Fútbol, founded in 1923 in the small town of Villarreal, Spain, has defied expectations by rising from humble beginnings to become a formidable force in European football. The club, affectionately known as "El Submarino Amarillo" (The Yellow Submarine) for their distinctive yellow jerseys, achieved their greatest triumph by winning the UEFA Europa League in 2021, defeating Manchester United in a dramatic penalty shootout final. 

Under the leadership of the Roig family since 1997, Villarreal has established itself as a model for sustainable football development, emphasizing youth academy excellence and strategic player development while maintaining strong community ties to their 50,000-person hometown. Currently competing in La Liga, the club consistently punches above its weight, regularly qualifying for European competitions and earning respect as one of Spain's most well-managed and competitive teams.
extra_info:
Villarreal has often been touted as an example of a small but successful club, having challenged Real Madrid, Barcelona, and Valencia for trophies over the last decade. The club plays in La Liga and finished 5th in the 2022-23 season. They are UEFA Europa League winners and UEFA Intertoto Cup winners.

— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Villarreal_CF#Current_technical_staff —
Known as 'El Submarino Amarillo' (The Yellow Submarine), the club won the UEFA Europa League in 2021 and has been a regular participant in European competitions.

name: Stevenage
bio:
Stevenage Football Club (known as Stevenage Borough Football Club until 2010) is a professional association football club based in the town of Stevenage, Hertfordshire, England. The club competes in EFL League One, the third tier of the English football league system. They play their home games at Broadhall Way in Stevenage.

Founded in 1976 following the demise of the town's former club, they joined the United Counties League in 1980 and enjoyed instant success; winning the United Counties League Division One and the United Counties League Cup in the club's first year in the competition. Following three promotions in four seasons in the early 1990s, the club were promoted to the Conference National in 1994. Despite winning the league in the 1995–96 season, the club were denied promotion to the Football League due to insufficient ground facilities. Stevenage were finally promoted to the Football League after winning the Conference Premier in the 2009–10 season. On securing Football League status, the club dropped the word 'Borough' from its title. Stevenage earned back-to-back promotions when they beat Torquay United 1–0 at Old Trafford in the 2010–11 play-off final.

The club has also enjoyed success in national cup competitions, becoming the first team to win a competitive final at the new Wembley Stadium in 2007, beating Kidderminster Harriers 3–2 to lift the FA Trophy in front of a competition record crowd of 53,262. The club won the competition again in 2009.

name: Lincoln City
bio:
Lincoln City Football Club are a professional association football club based in the city of Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England. The team compete in League One, the third tier of the English football league system.
In 1982 and again in 1983, Lincoln narrowly missed out on promotion to the Second Division. In 1985, Lincoln were the opposition at Bradford City when the Bradford City stadium fire claimed the lives of 56 spectators – two of them, Bill Stacey and Jim West, were Lincoln fans, and subsequently these fans had the Stacey West stand named after them.

name: Bologna Football Club 1909 S.p.A.
bio:
Bologna Football Club 1909 S.p.A. is one of Italy's oldest and most storied football clubs, founded in 1909 and based in the Emilia-Romagna region. The Rossoblu achieved their greatest success during the 1930s and 1940s, winning seven Serie A titles (1925, 1929, 1936, 1937, 1939, 1941, 1964) and establishing themselves as a dominant force in Italian football's golden era. 

Known for their passionate fanbase and deep-rooted connection to Bologna's working-class culture, the club plays at the historic Stadio Renato Dall'Ara and maintains a fierce rivalry with nearby clubs. Currently competing in Serie A under the ownership of Joey Saputo, Bologna has experienced a resurgence in recent years, consistently maintaining top-flight status and working to reclaim their position among Italy's elite clubs.
extra_info: Bologna is the sixth most successful team in Serie A history, having won the Italian league championship seven times (1924-25, 1928-29, 1935-36, 1936-37, 1938-39, 1940-41, 1963-64). The club has also won the Coppa Italia three times, including recently in 2024-25, and claimed three Mitropa Cup titles. They are currently owned by Canadian company Saputo Inc.

name: Elche Club de Fútbol, S.A.D.
bio:
Elche Club de Fútbol, S.A.D., founded in 1923 in the city of Elche in the Valencia region, is a historic Spanish football club that has experienced both triumph and adversity throughout its nearly century-long existence. The club's greatest achievement came in 1969 when they reached the Copa del Rey final, and they have spent multiple seasons competing in Spain's top flight, La Liga, including recent promotions that demonstrated their resilience and ambition. Known for their passionate local support and deep connection to the Valencian community, Elche plays their home matches at the Estadio Martínez Valero, creating a distinctive culture rooted in regional pride and determination. 

Currently competing in La Liga after earning promotion from the Segunda División, the club continues to establish itself as a competitive force in Spanish football while maintaining strong ties to their working-class roots and local identity.
extra_info: The club reached La Liga for the first time in 1959 and achieved their best league finish of fifth place in the 1963-64 season. They were runners-up in the Copa del Rey in 1969, which remains one of their most notable achievements in Spanish football.

name: Wigan Athletic
bio:
Wigan Athletic Football Club is a professional association football club based in Wigan, Greater Manchester, England. The team competes in the EFL League One, the third level of the English football league system.  Founded in 1932, the club have played at the 25,000-seater DW Stadium since 1999, before which they played at Springfield Park. Their colours are blue and white stripes, although all-blue shirts have been common throughout the club's history. Wigan was elected to the Football League in 1978, and competed in the Premier League from 2005 to 2013.

Wigan won the 2012–13 FA Cup with a 1–0 victory against Manchester City at Wembley Stadium when Ben Watson scored the winning goal in stoppage time, and the club also reached the League Cup Final in 2006. They are three-time winners of League One (2003, 2016, and 2018) and twice winners of the EFL Trophy (1985 and 1999). They made their European debut in the 2013–14 UEFA Europa League.

On 1 July 2020, less than a month after a change of ownership, Wigan announced the club had gone into administration.

name: Eintracht Frankfurt
bio:
Eintracht Frankfurt e.V. is a German sports club based in Frankfurt, Hesse, that is best known for its association football club, currently playing in the Bundesliga, the top tier of the German football league system.

The club was founded in 1899 and have won one German championship, five DFB-Pokals and one UEFA Cup. Since 1925, their stadium has been the Waldstadion, which was renamed Commerzbank-Arena in 2005.

History
Club origins
The origins of the side go back to a pair of football clubs founded in 1899: Frankfurter Fußball-Club Viktoria von 1899 – regarded as the "original" football side in the club's history – and Frankfurter Fußball-Club Kickers von 1899. Both clubs were founding members of the new Nordkreis-Liga in 1909. These two teams merged in May 1911 to become Frankfurter Fußball Verein (Kickers-Viktoria), an instant success, taking three league titles from 1912 to 1914 in the Nordkreis-Liga and qualifying for the Southern German championship in each of those seasons. In turn, Frankfurter FV joined the gymnastics club Frankfurter Turngemeinde von 1861 to form TuS Eintracht Frankfurt von 1861 in 1920. (The German word Eintracht means 'harmony, concord,' and Eintracht X is the equivalent of English X United in the names of sports teams.)

name: VfL Wolfsburg
bio:
VfL Wolfsburg, founded in 1945 in the German city of Wolfsburg, is a professional football club that competes in the Bundesliga and is uniquely owned by automotive giant Volkswagen. The club achieved its greatest success in 2009 when they captured their first and only Bundesliga title under coach Felix Magath, and they have also claimed the DFB-Pokal (German Cup) once in 2015. Known for their strong financial backing and modern facilities, Wolfsburg has established itself as a consistent European competitor, regularly participating in Champions League and Europa League competitions. 

Currently, the club maintains its position as a mid-to-upper table Bundesliga side, continuing to develop both domestic and international talent while representing the industrial heritage of their Volkswagen-company town.

name: AFC Wimbledon
bio:
AFC Wimbledon is an English professional football club, based in Merton, London, which has played in League One, the third tier of the English football league system, since winning promotion in 2016. The club's home stadium is Plough Lane.

The club was founded in 2002 by former supporters of Wimbledon F.C. after the Football Association allowed that club to relocate to Milton Keynes in Buckinghamshire, about 60 miles (97 km) north of Wimbledon. Most of the Wimbledon F.C. supporters were very strongly opposed to moving the club so far away from Wimbledon, feeling that a club transplanted to a distant location would no longer represent Wimbledon or the club's historic legacy and tradition. Wimbledon F.C. moved in 2003 and formally changed the name of the club to Milton Keynes Dons in 2004.

When AFC Wimbledon was formed, it affiliated to both the London and Surrey Football Associations, and entered the Premier Division of the Combined Counties League, the ninth tier of English football. The club has since been promoted six times in 13 seasons, going from the ninth tier (Combined Counties Premier) to the third (League One).

AFC Wimbledon currently hold the record for the longest unbeaten run of league matches in English senior football, having played 78 consecutive league games without a defeat between February 2003 and December 2004. They are the first club formed in the 21st century to make it into the Football League.

The club was initially based at Kingsmeadow, a ground bought from and then shared with Isthmian League club Kingstonian F.C. until 2017, and with Chelsea F.C. Women from 2017. In November 2020, the club moved to Plough Lane, a new stadium on the site of the defunct Wimbledon Greyhound Stadium, only 250 yards away from the original Plough Lane, Wimbledon F.C.'s home until 1991. The new stadium has an initial capacity of 9,300, with the option of expansion to a maximum 20,000 at a later date.

name: Futbol Club Barcelona
bio:
Futbol Club Barcelona, founded in 1899, is one of the world's most prestigious soccer clubs and a cornerstone of Catalonian identity, playing their home matches at the iconic Camp Nou stadium. The club has achieved unparalleled success with 27 La Liga titles, 31 Copa del Rey trophies, and 5 UEFA Champions League victories, while pioneering the distinctive "tiki-taka" playing style that emphasizes possession-based football and technical excellence. 

Barcelona's famous La Masia academy has produced legendary talents including Lionel Messi, Xavi, and Andrés Iniesta, embodying the club's motto "Més que un club" (More than a club) through its deep cultural significance and commitment to Catalonian values. Currently competing in La Liga, Barcelona continues to rebuild under their recent leadership while maintaining their status as one of global football's most influential and widely supported institutions.
extra_info: Barcelona is tied with Real Madrid as the most successful clubs in Spain with 78 official titles, including 22 La Liga titles, 4 UEFA Champions League trophies, and numerous other domestic and international honors. The club has produced a record number of Ballon d'Or winners (10), with 8 won by Lionel Messi. In 2009, Barcelona became the first club to win the sextuple, winning all six competitions in a single year. The team under Pep Guardiola is considered by many to be the greatest team of all time.

name: Real Club Deportivo Mallorca, S.A.D.
bio:
Real Club Deportivo Mallorca, founded in 1916 in Palma, Mallorca, is a historic Spanish football club that has established itself as a resilient force in Spanish football despite being based outside the mainland. The club achieved its greatest success during the late 1990s and early 2000s under coach Héctor Cúper, reaching the UEFA Cup Winners' Cup final in 1999 and consistently competing in European competitions while maintaining La Liga status for over a decade. Known for their passionate local fanbase and their iconic red-and-black striped jerseys, Mallorca has cultivated a strong island identity that emphasizes community connection and the development of local talent alongside experienced players. 

After experiencing relegation and financial difficulties in the 2010s, the club has successfully returned to La Liga and continues to compete at Spain's top level, representing the pride and determination of the Balearic Islands in elite Spanish football.
extra_info: The club is owned by Robert Sarver and finished 15th in La Liga in the 2023-24 season. Notable current players include Kosovo striker Vedat Muriqi, Serbian goalkeeper Predrag Rajković, and Spanish midfielder Sergi Darder. The team's nicknames reflect their vermillion colors and local Balearic identity.

name: 1. Fußballclub Heidenheim 1846 e.V.
bio:
1. FC Heidenheim 1846 is a German football club based in Heidenheim an der Brenz, Baden-Württemberg, founded in 1846 as a gymnastics club before transitioning to football. The club experienced a remarkable rise through Germany's football pyramid over the past two decades, climbing from the lower amateur divisions to achieve promotion to the Bundesliga for the first time in their history at the end of the 2023-24 season. Known for their disciplined, hard-working style of play and strong community connection in their small city of approximately 50,000 residents, 

Heidenheim has built a reputation as an efficient, well-run organization that maximizes limited resources. As one of the smallest cities ever to host Bundesliga football, FC Heidenheim represents the quintessential underdog story in German football, competing in the top flight while maintaining their grassroots identity and close ties to their local fanbase.
extra_info: The club achieved its greatest success in 2023-24 when it qualified to the UEFA Conference League in its first ever Bundesliga season. They finished 16th in the 2024-25 season. The club was founded in 1846, making it one of Germany's oldest football clubs.

name: Port Vale
bio:
Port Vale Football Club is a football club in Burslem, Stoke-on-Trent, England, which plays in League One, the third tier of the English football league system.

Port Vale is one of the few English league clubs not to be named after a geographical location, their name being a reference to the valley of ports on the Trent and Mersey Canal. They were founder members of the Second Division in 1892 and of the Fourth Division in 1958. After playing at the Athletic Ground in Cobridge and The Old Recreation Ground in Hanley, the club returned to Burslem when Vale Park was opened in 1950. Outside the ground is a statue to Roy Sproson who played 842 competitive games for the club.

The club greatest success was in the 1950s when they were FA Cup semi-finalists in 1954 and won two divisional titles with Freddie Steele's "Iron Curtain" defence and then Norman Low's more attacking style. John Rudge was manager from 1983 to 1999; under his leadership the club reached eighth in the second tier in the 1996–97 season. Since his reign the club have declined, slipping into the fourth tier whilst entering administration in 2003 and 2012. The decline was arrested when Paul Wildes and Norman Smurthwaite bought the club out of Administration in 2012 and manager Micky Adams achieved automatic promotion from League Two in the 2012–13 season.

The club's traditional rivals are Stoke City, and games between the two are known as the Potteries derby. However, with Stoke City in the Premier League, rivalry has increased between Port Vale and other clubs, including Burton Albion, Macclesfield Town, Shrewsbury Town, Walsall, Wrexham, and Crewe Alexandra.

name: Real Madrid Club de Fútbol
bio:
Real Madrid Club de Fútbol, founded in 1902 in Madrid, Spain, stands as one of the most prestigious and successful football clubs in world history. The club has won a record 14 UEFA Champions League titles and 35 La Liga championships, establishing itself as a dominant force both domestically and internationally. Known for its "Galáctico" policy of signing world-class superstars and playing at the iconic Santiago Bernabéu Stadium, Real Madrid has cultivated a global fanbase and a culture of excellence that attracts the finest players from around the world. 

Currently competing at the highest level of Spanish football, Los Blancos continue to be a major contender for all major trophies while maintaining their status as one of the most valuable sports franchises globally.
extra_info: Real Madrid was voted by FIFA as the most successful club of the 20th century, having won a record 36 La Liga titles, 20 Copa del Rey cups, 13 Spanish Supercups, and a record 15 UEFA Champions League titles. The club was a founding member of FIFA and the now-defunct G-14 group of Europe's leading football clubs. Notable current players include Kylian Mbappé, Vinícius Júnior, Jude Bellingham, and captain Luka Modrić.

name: Club Atlético Osasuna
bio:
Club Atlético Osasuna, founded in 1920 in Pamplona, Navarre, is a historic Spanish football club that has become synonymous with resilience and regional pride throughout its century-long existence. The club's most notable achievement came during the 2004-05 season when they reached the Copa del Rey final, while they have spent the majority of their history fluctuating between Spain's first and second divisions, demonstrating remarkable persistence in maintaining their competitive status. Known for their passionate fanbase and deep connection to Navarrese culture, Osasuna plays at the iconic El Sadar stadium, where their supporters create one of La Liga's most authentic and intimidating atmospheres. 

Currently competing in La Liga after their promotion in 2019, Los Rojillos continue to embody the fighting spirit that has defined their identity, consistently punching above their weight against Spain's footballing giants.
extra_info: The club finished 9th in La Liga in the 2024-25 season. They have rivalries with Real Zaragoza, Real Madrid, and Basque clubs particularly Athletic Bilbao. Notable current players include Croatian striker Ante Budimir and experienced forward Raúl García.

name: València Club de Fútbol, S.A.D.
bio: València Club de Fútbol, S.A.D., founded in 1919, is one of Spain's most historically successful football clubs, based in the vibrant city of Valencia on Spain's Mediterranean coast. The club has claimed six La Liga titles, eight Copa del Rey trophies, and notably won the UEFA Cup twice in 2004 and 2008 under Rafael Benítez, establishing themselves as a formidable force in both domestic and European competitions. Known for their passionate fanbase and the iconic Mestalla stadium, Valencia has cultivated a rich footballing culture that emphasizes attacking play and has produced numerous Spanish national team players throughout their history. Currently competing in La Liga, the club continues to develop young talent through their renowned academy while striving to return to their former glory days of challenging for major honors.
extra_info: Valencia have won six La Liga titles, seven Copa del Rey trophies, two Fairs Cups, one UEFA Cup, one UEFA Cup Winners' Cup and two UEFA Super Cups. They reached two UEFA Champions League finals in a row, losing to Real Madrid in 2000 and Bayern Munich on penalties in 2001. Valencia were also members of the G-14 group of leading European football clubs and have reached seven major European finals, winning four of them.

name: Levante UD
bio:
Levante Unión Deportiva, S.A.D. (Spanish: , Valencian: Llevant Unió Esportiva ) is a Spanish football club in Valencia, in the namesake autonomous community.

Founded on 9 September 1909, Levante play in Segunda División, holding home games at Ciutat de València Stadium.

name: AFC Bournemouth
bio: AFC Bournemouth is an English professional football club based in Bournemouth, Dorset. Founded in 1899 as Boscombe FC, the club has experienced a remarkable rise through the English football pyramid, reaching the Premier League for the first time in 2015 under Eddie Howe. Known as 'The Cherries', they play at the Vitality Stadium and are notable for being one of the smallest towns to have a Premier League team.

name: Aston Villa
bio: Aston Villa Football Club, founded in 1874 in Birmingham, England, is one of the oldest and most storied clubs in English football, having won seven First Division titles and the European Cup in 1982. The club boasts a rich heritage as one of the founding members of both the Football League in 1888 and the Premier League in 1992, with their iconic claret and blue colors representing over 150 years of tradition at their historic Villa Park home. Known for developing exceptional talent through their renowned academy and maintaining a passionate, loyal fanbase called the "Villans," Aston Villa embodies a strong community spirit and working-class identity rooted in Birmingham's industrial heritage. After returning to the Premier League in 2019 following a brief stint in the Championship, Villa has re-established itself as a competitive force under ambitious ownership, consistently finishing in mid-table while building toward European competition.

name: Reading
bio:
Reading Football Club is an English professional football club, based in Reading, that plays in the Championship. The club competed in the 2012–13 Premier League season, having gained promotion at the end of the 2011–12 season, after winning the Championship. Reading is competing in the Championship again this season having been relegated after one year back in the top flight.

Reading are nicknamed The Royals, due to Reading's location in the Royal County of Berkshire, though they were previously known as The Biscuitmen, due to the town's association with Huntley and Palmers. Established in 1871, the club is one of the oldest teams in England, but did not join The Football League until 1920, and had never played in the top tier of English football league system before the 2006–07 season.

The club played at Elm Park for 102 years between 1896 to 1998. The club moved in 1998 to the new Madejski Stadium, which is named after the club's Co chairman Sir John Madejski.

The club holds the record for the number of successive league wins at the start of a season, with a total of 13 wins at the start of the 1985–86 Third Division campaign and also the record for the number of points gained in a professional league season with 106 points in the 2005–06 Football League Championship campaign. Reading finished eighth in their first ever season as a top flight club.

name: Girona Futbol Club, S.A.D.
bio:
Girona Futbol Club, S.A.D. is a Spanish football club founded in 1930 and based in Girona, Catalonia, that has experienced a remarkable transformation from lower-division obscurity to La Liga prominence. The club achieved its historic first promotion to Spain's top flight in 2017 and, after a brief stint in the second division, returned to La Liga with renewed ambition backed by the City Football Group's investment since 2022. Known for their distinctive red and white striped jerseys and strong Catalan identity, Girona plays at the 14,624-capacity Estadi Montilivi and has built a reputation for attractive, attacking football under innovative coaching. 

Currently competing in La Liga, the club has established itself as a competitive force in Spanish football, representing the proud footballing tradition of Catalonia while pursuing sustainable growth and European aspirations.
extra_info: Girona finished 3rd in La Liga during the 2023-24 season, their best ever finish in the top flight. The club is owned by City Football Group (47%), Marcelo Claure (35%), and Girona Football Group (16%). Notable players include Ukrainian forwards Viktor Tsigankov and Artem Dovbyk, and veteran Uruguayan striker Cristhian Stuani.

name: Blackburn Rovers
bio:
Blackburn Rovers Football Club, founded in 1875 and based in Blackburn, Lancashire, is one of England's most historic football clubs and a founding member of both the Football League in 1888 and the Premier League in 1992. The club achieved legendary status by becoming Premier League champions in the 1994-95 season under manager Kenny Dalglish, marking one of the most remarkable title wins in English football history, while also boasting six FA Cup victories between 1884 and 1928. Known for their passionate working-class support and the iconic blue and white halved shirts, Rovers have cultivated a proud tradition at their home ground Ewood Park, fostering a strong connection with the local community. 

Currently competing in the EFL Championship, the club continues to strive for promotion back to the Premier League while maintaining their reputation as a well-established institution in English football.

name: Brentford
bio: Brentford Football Club, founded in 1889 and based in West London, is a historic English club that achieved a remarkable milestone in 2021 by securing promotion to the Premier League for the first time in 74 years. Known as "The Bees," the club has built a reputation for innovative data-driven recruitment and tactical approaches under manager Thomas Frank, while maintaining strong community ties and playing at their modern Brentford Community Stadium since 2020. Their greatest historical achievement came in 1946 when they reached the First Division (now Premier League), and they have consistently punched above their weight in recent decades, earning promotion from League One in 2014 and the Championship in 2021. Currently established as a competitive Premier League side, Brentford has successfully maintained their top-flight status while continuing to embody their motto "Together" through their close-knit community culture and fearless approach against traditionally larger clubs.

name: Bristol City
bio:
Bristol City Football Club, founded in 1894 and based in Bristol, England, is a professional soccer club that competes in the EFL Championship, the second tier of English football. The Robins achieved their greatest success by winning the Second Division title in 1906 and reaching the FA Cup Final in 1909, while also claiming the Football League Trophy in 1986 and 2003.
