Known for their passionate fanbase at Ashton Gate Stadium and their distinctive red home kit, the club has built a strong community presence in Bristol and maintains fierce local rivalries, particularly with Cardiff City in the Severnside Derby. 

Currently managed by Roy Hodgson, Bristol City continues to compete in the Championship with aspirations of promotion to the Premier League, having established themselves as a competitive force in England's second division.

name: Charlton Athletic
bio: Founded in 1905 and based in South East London, Charlton Athletic Football Club has established itself as a resilient community-focused institution with a rich history spanning over a century. The Addicks achieved their greatest success by winning the FA Cup in 1947 and have spent significant periods in England's top flight, including a memorable seven-year stint in the Premier League from 2000-2007. Known for their passionate fanbase and strong community ties, Charlton has overcome numerous financial challenges and ownership changes, with supporters famously campaigning for the club's return to The Valley stadium in the 1990s. Currently competing in League One following relegation from the Championship, the club continues to rebuild while maintaining its reputation as a family-oriented club with deep roots in South London football culture.

name: Real Oviedo, S.A.D.
bio: Real Oviedo, S.A.D. is a historic Spanish football club founded in 1926 and based in Oviedo, Asturias, with their home stadium being the Carlos Tartiere. The club enjoyed their golden era in the 1990s, spending several seasons in La Liga and notably reaching the UEFA Cup in 1991-92, while also claiming the Segunda División title in 1987-88. Known for their passionate fanbase called "Carbayones" and their deep connection to Asturian culture, Real Oviedo has built a reputation for resilience, having overcome financial difficulties in 2012 through an innovative crowdfunding campaign supported by fans worldwide. Currently competing in the Segunda División, the club continues to strive for a return to Spain's top flight while maintaining their status as one of northern Spain's most beloved football institutions.
extra_info: Real Oviedo plays at the Estadio Carlos Tartiere, which opened on 30 September 2000 and is the largest sports stadium in Asturias. The club was promoted to La Liga after finishing 3rd in the 2024-25 Segunda División and winning the play-offs. The club is owned by Mexican groups Grupo Pachuca (51%) and Grupo Carso (20%).

name: Cardiff City
bio:
Cardiff City Football Club is a professional association football club based in Cardiff, Wales. It currently competes in EFL League One, the third tier of the English football league system  The club played in the Premier League for the first time in the 2013–14 season, at the end of which they were relegated. They play their home games at the Cardiff City Stadium, after moving from Ninian Park in 2009.

The club was founded as Riverside A.F.C. in 1899 and is the only club from outside England to have won the FA Cup, which they won in 1927. The club won the Football League Championship title in the 2012–13 season and were promoted to the top flight for the first time in 51 years. This followed two lost national cup finals, the 2008 FA Cup Final against Portsmouth and the 2012 Football League Cup Final against Liverpool, the latter being settled by a penalty shootout.

In 2012, Cardiff City was rebranded by the club's Malaysian owner, Vincent Tan. This included a controversial change of the club's home colours from blue to red.

Riverside A.F.C. was formed in 1899 as a way of keeping players from the Riverside Cricket Club together and in shape during the winter months. Their first season saw them playing friendlies against local sides at their Sophia Gardens ground, but in 1900 they joined the Cardiff & District League for their first competitive season. In 1905 Cardiff was granted city status by King Edward VII, and as a result the club put in a request to change their name to Cardiff City, but the request was turned down as they were deemed to be not playing at a high enough level. To combat this they arranged to join the South Wales Amateur League in 1907 and the following year they were granted permission to change the name of the club to Cardiff City.

Interest in the club began to rise during this time, but they were forced to turn down the opportunity to join the newly formed Southern League Second Division due to the lack of facilities at their Sophia Gardens ground. Over the next two years Cardiff welcomed many of Britain's top sides to Cardiff, including Middlesbrough, Bristol City and Crystal Palace, with the matches being played at various grounds in Cardiff and nearby towns. The club would eventually move into their new ground, Ninian Park, in 1910. The club made its first signing the following year with the acquisition of Jack Evans from fellow Welsh club Cwmparc FC.

With the new ground in place, Cardiff joined the Southern League Second Division, and Bartley Wilson was quick to hire the club's first manager in Davy McDougall, who became player-manager. Their first match was a 2–1 defeat to Aston Villa, in which new signing Evans scored the only Cardiff goal. They went on to finish in fourth place in their first year in the league. They stayed in the division for the next decade, apart from when the league was suspended due to the outbreak of World War I.

name: Sevilla FC
bio:
Sevilla FC, founded in 1890, is one of Spain's most prestigious football clubs based in Andalusia and a founding member of La Liga. The club has achieved remarkable success both domestically and internationally, winning one La Liga title, five Copa del Rey trophies, and a record seven UEFA Europa League titles, establishing themselves as the most successful team in European secondary competition history. Known for their passionate fanbase, rich Andalusian culture, and distinctive playing style, Sevilla plays at the iconic Ramón Sánchez-Pizjuán Stadium and maintains a fierce rivalry with Real Betis in the Seville Derby. 

Currently competing in La Liga's top tier, Sevilla consistently qualifies for European competitions and remains a formidable force in both Spanish and continental football.
extra_info: The club participated in the 2020-21 La Liga season and reached the third round of the Copa del Rey. They have maintained their position in Spain's top flight for two decades.

name: Everton
bio:
Everton Football Club, founded in 1878 and based in Liverpool, England, is one of the founding members of the Football League and has spent more seasons in England's top flight than any other club. The Toffees have achieved significant success throughout their history, winning nine league titles, five FA Cups, and the European Cup Winners' Cup in 1985, establishing themselves as one of English football's most storied institutions. 

Known for their passionate fanbase at Goodison Park and the club motto "Nil Satis Nisi Optimum" (Nothing but the best is good enough), Everton maintains a fierce rivalry with cross-city neighbors Liverpool FC in the famous Merseyside Derby. Currently competing in the Premier League, the club is undergoing a period of transition while preparing to move to a new state-of-the-art stadium at Bramley-Moore Dock, aiming to return to their former glory days.

name: Hull City
bio:
Hull City Association Football Club, founded in 1904 and known as "The Tigers," is based in Kingston upon Hull, East Yorkshire, and currently competes in the English Football League Championship. The club achieved its greatest success in 2014 when they reached the FA Cup Final at Wembley Stadium, narrowly losing 3-2 to Arsenal after extra time, which remains one of the most memorable cup final performances by a Championship side. 

Hull City has experienced significant fluctuations between divisions throughout its history, including two brief spells in the Premier League (2008-2010 and 2013-2016), and has built a reputation for resilient, hardworking football that reflects the industrial heritage of their hometown. 

The club plays at the MKM Stadium and continues to strive for promotion back to the Premier League while maintaining strong community ties with their passionate fanbase in East Yorkshire.

name: Leeds United
bio:
Leeds United Football Club is a professional football club based in Leeds, West Yorkshire, England. The club competes in the Premier League, the top tier of English football.  The club was formed in 1919 following the disbanding of Leeds City F.C. by the Football League and took over their Elland Road stadium. 

Most of their history has been spent playing in the first tier; their longest continuous spell inside the first tier was a period of 18 years between 1964 and 1982, while their longest period outside of it spanned 16 years between 2004 and 2020. They have won three English league titles, one FA Cup, one League Cup, two Charity/Community Shields and two Inter-Cities Fairs Cups. The club reached the 1975 European Cup Final, losing to Bayern Munich after some controversial refereeing decisions. Leeds reached the semi-finals of the tournament's successor, the Champions League in 2001. The club were also runners-up in the European Cup Winners Cup final in 1973. The majority of the honours were won under the management of Don Revie in the 1960s and 1970s.

Leeds play in all-white kits at home matches. The club's badge features the White Rose of York. The club's anthem is Marching on Together. Leeds share rivalries with Manchester United and Chelsea, as well as with local teams such as Huddersfield Town, Bradford City and Sheffield Wednesday.

name: Liverpool FC
bio: Liverpool Football Club is a professional football club based in Liverpool, England. Founded in 1892, the club competes in the Premier League and plays home matches at Anfield. Liverpool is one of the most successful clubs in English football history, having won 19 league titles, 8 FA Cups, 10 League Cups, and 6 European Cup/Champions League titles. Known as 'The Reds', the club has a passionate global fanbase and is famous for the Anfield atmosphere and the anthem 'You'll Never Walk Alone'.
extra_info:
— From https://www.liverpoolfc.com/team/mens —
One of the most successful clubs in English football history with 19 league titles and 6 European Cup/Champions League titles. Known for their passionate fanbase and the famous 'You'll Never Walk Alone' anthem.

name: Manchester United
bio:
Manchester United, founded in 1878 and based in Manchester, England, is one of the most successful and globally recognized football clubs in history, having won a record 20 English league titles, 12 FA Cups, and three European Cup/Champions League trophies. 

The club reached its golden era under legendary manager Sir Alex Ferguson (1986-2013), capturing 13 Premier League titles and establishing a culture built on attacking football, youth development, and an unwavering winning mentality. Known as the "Red Devils," United boasts one of the world's largest fanbases and plays at the iconic Old Trafford stadium, often called the "Theatre of Dreams." 

While the club has faced challenges returning to its former dominance since Ferguson's retirement, Manchester United remains a powerhouse in English football and continues to compete at the highest levels domestically and in European competition.

name: RC Celta de Vigo
bio:
RC Celta de Vigo, founded in 1923 in the Galician city of Vigo, is a historic Spanish football club that competes in La Liga and calls the Estadio Balaídos home. The club's greatest achievement came during the late 1990s and early 2000s under manager Claudio Ranieri, when they consistently competed in European competitions and reached the UEFA Cup semi-finals in 2000, while also producing legendary players like Iago Aspas. Known for their passionate Galician identity and attacking style of play, Celta has cultivated a devoted fanbase that creates an electric atmosphere at Balaídos, earning recognition as one of Spain's most atmospheric stadiums. 

Currently established as a mid-table La Liga side, the club continues to develop young talent while maintaining their reputation for entertaining football and fierce rivalry with fellow Galician club Deportivo La Coruña.
extra_info: The club participates in both La Liga and Copa del Rey competitions. They play their home matches at Balaídos stadium.

name: Middlesbrough
bio:
Middlesbrough Football Club also known as Boro, are an English football club based in Middlesbrough, who play in the Football League Championship. Formed in 1876, they have played at the Riverside Stadium since August 1995, their third ground since turning professional in 1889. Their longest-serving home was Ayresome Park, where they played for 92 years, from 1903 to 1995.

They were one of the founding members of the Premier League in 1992. The club's main rivals are Sunderland and Newcastle United, however the club also takes part in Yorkshire derbies with several other Yorkshire clubs; most notably Leeds United.

The club's highest league finish to date was third in the 1913–14 season and they have only spent two seasons outside of the Football League's top two divisions. The club came close to folding in 1986 after experiencing severe financial difficulties before the club was saved by a consortium led by then board member and later chairman Steve Gibson. Middlesbrough were controversially deducted three points for failing to fulfil a fixture against Blackburn Rovers during the 1996–97 Premier League season and were subsequently relegated. They were promoted the following season and spent eleven consecutive seasons in the top division before relegation. Middlesbrough won the League Cup in 2004, the club's first and only major trophy. They reached the 2006 UEFA Cup Final in May 2006 but were beaten by Spanish side Sevilla. On 24 May 2009, Middlesbrough were relegated to the Championship, failing to extend their 11-year stay in the Premier League.

The club's traditional kit is red with white detailing. The various crests throughout the club's history, the most recent of which was adopted in May 2007, incorporate a lion rampant.

name: Millwall
bio:
Millwall Football Club is an English professional football club based in New Cross, south-east London, that plays in the Football League Championship, the second tier of English football. Founded as Millwall Rovers in 1885, the club has retained its name despite having last played in the Millwall area of the Isle of Dogs in 1910. From then until 1993 the club played at The Den, a now-demolished stadium in New Cross, before moving to its current home stadium nearby, also called The Den.

The traditional club crest is a lion rampant, referred to in the team's nickname "The Lions". Millwall's traditional kit consists of blue shirts, white shorts and blue socks. Millwall have a long-standing rivalry with West Ham United. The local derby between the two sides has been contested almost 100 times since 1899. In the media, Millwall's supporters have often been associated with hooliganism, with numerous films having been made fictionalising their notoriety. The fans are renowned for their chant "No one likes us, we don't care" which is sung to the tune of Sailing by Rod Stewart.

In 2004, the team reached the final of the FA Cup and, in doing so, qualified for the UEFA Cup the following season, playing in Europe for the first time in their history. The club has also reached FA Cup semi-finals on another four occasions, in 1900, 1903, 1937 and 2013. Millwall have spent the majority of their existence competing in the second or third tier of the Football League. The team spent two seasons in the top flight between 1988–90, in which the club achieved its highest ever finish of tenth place in the First Division. Based on all results during the club's 87 seasons in the Football League from 1920–21 to 2013–14, Millwall are ranked as the 40th most successful club in English football.

name: Newcastle United
bio:
Newcastle United Football Club (often abbreviated to NUFC) is an English professional association football club based in Newcastle upon Tyne. The club was founded in 1892 by the merger of Newcastle East End and Newcastle West End, and has played at its current home ground, St James' Park, ever since. The ground was developed into an all-seater stadium in the mid-1990s and now has a capacity of 52,405. The club has been a member of the Premier League for all but two years of the competition's history, and has never dropped below English football's second tier since joining the Football League in 1893. In 2007, long term chairman and owner Sir John Hall sold his share in the club to Mike Ashley.

They have won four League Championship titles and six FA Cups, as well as the 1969 Inter-Cities Fairs Cup and the 2006 UEFA Intertoto Cup. Newcastle United has the ninth highest total of major honours won by an English club. The club's most successful period was between 1904 and 1910, when they won an FA Cup and three of their First Division titles. The club is the twentieth richest club in the world in terms of annual revenue, generating €115.3m in 2012. Historically, Newcastle’s highest placing was fifth in the world.

They have a fierce local rivalry with Sunderland, and the two clubs have engaged in the Tyne–Wear derby since 1898. The club's traditional kit colours are black and white striped shirts, black shorts and black socks. Their traditional crest takes elements of the city coat of arms, which features two seahorses.

name: Norwich City
bio:
Norwich City Football Club is a professional football club based in Norwich, Norfolk, England. The club competes in the Championship, the second tier of English football.  The club was founded in 1902. Since 1935, Norwich have played their home games at Carrow Road and have a long-standing and fierce rivalry with East Anglian rivals Ipswich Town, with whom they have contested the East Anglian derby 134 times since 1902. The fans' song "On the Ball, City" is the oldest football chant in the world, written in 1890 and still sung today.

Norwich have won the League Cup twice, in 1962 and 1985. The club's highest ever league finish came in 1992–93 when they finished third in the Premier League.

The club participates in characteristic yellow and green kits and are nicknamed The Canaries after the history of breeding the birds in the area (said to be introduced around the 16th century by a group of European immigrant weavers in the area known as "The Strangers").

name: Oxford United
bio: Oxford United Football Club, founded in 1893 and based in Oxford, Oxfordshire, has experienced both remarkable highs and challenging periods throughout its storied history. The club's golden era came in the 1980s under manager Jim Smith, when they achieved promotion from the Fourth Division to the First Division in successive seasons and captured their greatest triumph by winning the League Cup in 1986, defeating Queens Park Rangers 3-0 at Wembley Stadium. Known for their passionate fanbase and distinctive yellow and blue colors, Oxford United has cultivated a strong community identity and loyal following despite facing financial difficulties and relegation battles in later decades. Currently competing in League One (the third tier of English football), the U's continue to rebuild under ambitious ownership, striving to return to the Championship and recapture their former glory while maintaining their reputation as a resilient, community-focused club.

name: Portsmouth
bio:
Portsmouth Football Club, founded in 1898 and based on England's south coast, is one of only five English clubs to have won both the FA Cup and UEFA Cup, claiming FA Cup victories in 1939 and 2008 along with their UEFA Cup triumph in 2008. 

The club, affectionately known as "Pompey," enjoyed their most successful period in the early 2000s, reaching the Premier League and European competition while building a passionate fanbase renowned for their loyalty through both triumph and adversity. 

Despite facing severe financial difficulties that led to relegations and administration in the 2010s, Portsmouth has maintained its strong community identity and proud naval heritage, with supporters demonstrating unwavering dedication during the club's darkest periods. Currently competing in League One after their relegation from the Championship, Portsmouth continues to rebuild under fan ownership while preserving the traditions and fighting spirit that have defined the club throughout its 125-year history.

name: Queens Park Rangers
bio:
Queens Park Rangers Football Club, commonly abbreviated to QPR, is a professional association football club based in Shepherd's Bush, West London, England. The team currently compete in the EFL Championship, the second level of the English football league system.  Their honours include winning the League Cup in 1967, being runners-up in the old First Division in 1975–76 and reaching the final of the FA Cup in 1982, where they lost 1–0 to Tottenham Hotspur in a replay after they drew 1–1 in the initial final match.

Queens Park Rangers Football Club were founded in 1882 after the merger of Christchurch Rangers and St. Judes Institute, and their traditional colours are blue and white. In the early years after the club's formation in their original home of Queen's Park, games were played at many different grounds until finally the club settled into their current location at Loftus Road. Owing to their proximity to other west London clubs, QPR maintain long-standing rivalries with several other clubs in the area. The most notable of these are Chelsea, Fulham and Brentford, with whom they contest what are known as West London Derbies. Outside London, QPR also traditionally share rivalries with Watford, Luton and Cardiff, although in recent years these fixtures have become less prominent.

name: Sheffield United
bio:
Sheffield United Football Club is a professional football club in Sheffield, South Yorkshire, England, which competes in the Championship, the second tier of English football. They formed in 1889, as an offshoot of Sheffield United Cricket Club, and are nicknamed The Blades due to Sheffield's history of steel production. They have played home games at Bramall Lane since their formation.

Sheffield United won the original Football League in 1898 and the FA Cup in 1899, 1902, 1915 and 1925. They were beaten finalists in the FA Cup in 1901 and 1936, and reached the semi-finals in 1961, 1993, 1998, 2003 and 2014. They reached the semi-finals of the League Cup in 2003 and 2015.

Sheffield United were the first club in English football to achieve promotion from the newly formed Second Division to the First Division in 1892–93. The club was also a founder member of the Premier League in the 1992–93 season, during which they scored the first ever goal of the Premier League era. Brian Deane was the scorer of the first goal in a 2–1 win at Bramall Lane, against the eventual champions, Manchester United.

Since the club's formation, they have played in tiers 1 through to 4 of the football league. They are one of only four clubs to have finished as champions of each of the top four tiers of the football league. Most of the club's history however has been spent in tier 1.

For most of the club's history they have played in red and white striped shirts with black shorts. Their closest rivals are Sheffield Wednesday, with whom they contest the Steel City derby.

name: Getafe Club de Fútbol S.A.D.
bio:
Getafe Club de Fútbol S.A.D. is a Spanish professional football club founded in 1946 and based in Getafe, a suburb south of Madrid, that has established itself as a resilient competitor in La Liga since earning promotion to Spain's top flight in 2004. The club achieved its greatest success under manager Michael Laudrup and later Quique Sánchez Flores, reaching the Copa del Rey final in 2007 and making a memorable run to the UEFA Cup quarter-finals in 2008, defeating Ajax and Bayern Munich along the way. Known for their pragmatic, defensive style of play and fierce determination, Getafe has built a reputation as a difficult opponent for Spain's elite clubs, often punching above their weight despite operating with one of La Liga's smaller budgets. 

Currently managed by José Bordalás, the club continues to compete in La Liga and has become synonymous with their never-say-die attitude and ability to secure crucial results against more favored opposition.
extra_info: Getafe participated in the top level of Spanish football for twelve years between 2004 and 2016. The club plays at Coliseum Alfonso Pérez stadium which was founded in 1998. Their main rivalries include CD Leganés due to close proximity, and they maintain friendly rivalries with both Real Madrid and Atlético Madrid.

name: Southampton
bio:
Southampton Football Club, founded in 1885, is a historic English football club based on the south coast that has established itself as a prominent force in English football over more than a century. The Saints achieved their greatest success during the 1970s and 1980s, winning the FA Cup in 1976 and reaching the pinnacle of English football by finishing second in the First Division in 1984, while also competing in European competitions. 

Known for their exceptional youth academy that has produced world-class talents including Gareth Bale, Luke Shaw, and Theo Walcott, Southampton has built a reputation for developing homegrown players and playing attractive, attacking football. Following relegation from the Premier League in 2023, the club currently competes in the Championship and is working to rebuild and return to England's top flight under their continued philosophy of nurturing young talent.

name: Swansea City
bio:
Swansea City Association Football Club is a Welsh professional football club based in Swansea, Wales. It competes in the Championship, the second tier of English football.
 Swansea City represent England when playing in European competitions, although they have represented Wales in the past. They play their home matches at the Liberty Stadium.

The club was founded in 1912 as Swansea Town and joined the Football League in 1921. The club changed their name in 1969, when it adopted the name Swansea City to reflect Swansea's new status as a city.

In 1981, the club were promoted to the original Football League First Division. It was during the following season they came close to winning the league title, but a decline then set near the season's end before finishing sixth, although a club record. It was from here the club suffered a relegation the season after, returning to the Football League Fourth Division a few seasons later, then narrowly avoided relegation to the Football Conference in 2003. Prior to playing home matches at the Liberty Stadium, the team had previously hosted at the Vetch Field. The Swansea City Supporters Society Ltd owns 20% of the club, with their involvement hailed by Supporters Direct as "the most high profile example of the involvement of a supporters' trust in the direct running of a club". The club's subsequent climb from the fourth division of English football to the top division is chronicled in the 2014 film, Jack to a King – The Swansea Story.

In 2011, Swansea were promoted to the English Premier League, becoming the first Welsh team to play in it since its formation in 1992. On 24 February 2013, Swansea beat Bradford City 5–0 to win the 2012–13 Football League Cup (the competition's highest ever winning margin for the final), winning the first major trophy in the club's history and qualifying for the 2013–14 UEFA Europa League, where they reach the Last 32 stage but lost over two legs to SSC Napoli.

name: Tottenham Hotspur
bio:
Tottenham Hotspur Football Club, commonly referred to as simply Tottenham or Spurs, is a professional football club based in Tottenham, North London, England. The club itself has stated that it should always be called "Tottenham Hotspur" or "Spurs", as Tottenham is the area of London and not the name of the club. It competes in the Premier League, the top tier of English football. The team have played their home matches in the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium since 2019, replacing their former home of White Hart Lane, which had been demolished to make way for the new stadium on the same site.

Founded in 1882, Tottenham Hotspur's emblem is a cockerel standing upon a football, with the Latin motto Audere est Facere ("to dare is to do"). The team have traditionally worn white shirts and navy blue shorts as their home kit since the 1898–99 season. Their training ground is on Hotspur Way in Bulls Cross, Enfield. After its inception, Tottenham won the FA Cup for the first time in 1901, the only non-League club to do so since the formation of the Football League in 1888. Tottenham was the first club in the 20th century to achieve the League and FA Cup Double, winning both competitions in the 1960–61 season. After successfully defending the FA Cup in 1962, in 1963 they became the first British club to win a UEFA club competition – the European Cup Winners' Cup. They were also the inaugural winners of the UEFA Cup in 1972, becoming the first British club to win two different major European trophies. They collected at least one major trophy in each of the six decades from the 1950s to 2000s, an achievement matched only by Manchester United.

In domestic football, Spurs have won two league titles, eight FA Cups, four League Cups, and seven FA Community Shields. In European football, they have won one European Cup Winners' Cup and three UEFA Cup/Europa League titles. Tottenham were also runners-up in the 2018–19 UEFA Champions League. They have a long-standing rivalry with nearby club Arsenal, with whom they contest the North London derby, as well as a contested rivalry with Chelsea. Tottenham is owned by ENIC Group, which purchased the club in 2001. The club was estimated to be worth £2.6 billion ($3.2 billion) in 2024, and it was the ninth-highest-earning football club in the world, with an annual revenue of £615 million in 2024.

name: Wolverhampton Wanderers
bio:
Wolverhampton Wanderers Football Club commonly referred to as Wolves, is a professional football club based in Wolverhampton, England. The club competes in the Premier League, the top tier of English football.
 The club was originally known as St. Luke's FC and was founded in 1877 and since 1889 has played at Molineux. 

Historically, Wolves have been highly influential, most notably as being founder members of the Football League, as well as having played an instrumental role in the establishment of the European Cup, later to become the UEFA Champions League. Having won the FA Cup twice before the outbreak of the First World War, they developed into one of England's leading clubs under the management of ex-player Stan Cullis after the Second World War, going on to win the league three times and the FA Cup twice more between 1949 and 1960. It was during this time that the European Cup competition was established, after the English press declared Wolves "Champions of the World" following their victories against numerous top European and World sides in some of British football's first live televised games.

Wolves have yet to match the successes of the Stan Cullis era, although, under Bill McGarry, they contested the first-ever UEFA Cup final in 1972 and won the 1974 League Cup, a trophy they lifted again six years later under John Barnwell. However, financial mismanagement in the 1980s led to the club's very existence being under threat as well as three consecutive relegations, before a revival and back-to-back promotions under manager Graham Turner and record goalscorer Steve Bull saw them finish the decade in the Second Division, winning the Football League Trophy along the way.

name: Watford
bio:
Watford Football Club, founded in 1881 and based in Hertfordshire, England, is a historic club that has experienced remarkable fluctuations between England's top divisions throughout its history. The Hornets' most notable achievements include reaching the FA Cup Final in 1984 and securing promotion to the Premier League on multiple occasions, most recently spending several seasons in the top flight between 2015-2022 under various ownership changes. 

Known for their passionate fanbase at Vicarage Road and their distinctive yellow kit, Watford has built a reputation for developing young talent and maintaining strong community ties despite frequent managerial changes. Currently competing in the Championship following relegation from the Premier League, the club continues to strive for a return to England's elite division while rebuilding under new leadership.

name: West Bromwich Albion
bio:
West Bromwich Albion Football Club commonly known as West Brom, The Baggies or The Albion, is a professional association football club based in West Bromwich, West Midlands, England. They compete in the EFL Championship, the second level of the English football league. The club was formed in 1878 and has played at its home ground, The Hawthorns, since 1900. 

Albion was one of the 12 founding members of the Football League in 1888, the first professional football league in the world. The club has spent the majority of its existence in the top tier of English football, where as of 2020–21 it has played for 82 seasons. The club has been champions of England once, in 1919–20, and has been runners-up twice. Albion teams have reached ten FA Cup finals and won the Cup on five occasions. The first win came in 1888, the year the league was founded, and the most recent in 1968, the club's last major trophy. It also won the Football League Cup at the first attempt in 1966. The club's longest continuous period in the top division spanned 24 years between 1949 and 1973, and from 1986 to 2002 it spent its longest ever spell out of the top division.

The team has played in navy blue and white stripes for most of the club's history, and the club badge features a throstle perched on a hawthorn branch. Albion has a number of long-standing rivalries with other West Midlands clubs, with its traditional rivals being Aston Villa and Wolves. Albion contests the Black Country derby with the latter.

name: West Ham United
bio:
West Ham United, founded in 1895 as Thames Ironworks FC in East London, has established itself as one of England's most storied football clubs with a rich working-class heritage rooted in the local community. The Hammers have achieved notable success including FA Cup victories in 1964, 1975, and 1980, a European Cup Winners' Cup triumph in 1965, and have produced legendary English players such as Bobby Moore, Geoff Hurst, and Martin Peters who were instrumental in England's 1966 World Cup victory. 

Known for their passionate fanbase and commitment to developing homegrown talent through their renowned academy, West Ham moved from their iconic Boleyn Ground to the London Stadium in 2016, marking a new era for the club. Currently competing in the Premier League, West Ham continues to punch above their weight under manager David Moyes, recently securing European qualification and maintaining their reputation as giant-killers capable of challenging England's elite clubs.

name: Wrexham
bio:
Wrexham Association Football Club, founded in 1864, is the world's third-oldest professional football club and Wales' oldest, based in Wrexham, North Wales. The Red Dragons have a rich history including 23 Welsh Cup victories and notable giant-killing runs in the FA Cup, most famously defeating Arsenal in 1992, while cultivating a passionate fanbase deeply rooted in their working-class community. 

Since their high-profile takeover by Hollywood actors Ryan Reynolds and Rob McConaughey in 2021, the club has experienced a remarkable renaissance, earning back-to-back promotions from the National League to League One. Currently competing in League One for the 2024-25 season, Wrexham has become a global phenomenon through the "Welcome to Wrexham" documentary series, transforming from a struggling non-league side into one of football's most watched and celebrated clubs.

name: Fulham
bio:
Fulham Football Club, founded in 1879, is London's oldest professional football club and currently competes in the Premier League from their historic home ground, Craven Cottage, situated on the banks of the River Thames. The Cottagers achieved their greatest success under manager Roy Hodgson, reaching the UEFA Europa League final in 2010, while also claiming the First Division title in 2001 and the Second Division championship in 1999. 

Known for their family-friendly atmosphere and traditional values, Fulham has cultivated a reputation as a club that punches above its weight, often featuring exciting, attacking football that has endeared them to neutrals across England. Under current manager Marco Silva, the club has established itself as a competitive Premier League side following their 2022 promotion, continuing their proud tradition of resilient football in one of the world's most prestigious leagues.

name: Real Betis
bio:
Real Betis Balompié, SAD, more commonly referred to as Real Betis is a Spanish football club based in Seville, in the autonomous community of Andalusia. Founded on 12 September 1907, it currently plays in the La Liga Segunda Division, having been relegated from the Primera Division in the 2013-14 season. It holds home games at Estadio Benito Villamarín in the south of the city.

Among other titles, the club – which in 1932 became the first Andalusian team to play in La Liga – won the Primera División in 1935 and the Copa del Rey in 1977 and 2005.

Both the King of Spain Juan Carlos I and his son Prince Felipe de Borbon are honorary members of the club. Maintaining an historic city rivalry with Sevilla FC, its motto is Viva el Betis manque pierda! ("Long live Betis even when they lose!").

name: Rayo Vallecano
bio:
Rayo Vallecano de Madrid, S.A.D. (Spanish pronunciation: ), often abbreviated to Rayo, is a Spanish football team based in Madrid, in the neighbourhood of Vallecas. Rayo was founded on 29 May 1924, and currently compete in La Liga. Home games are held at the 14,708-seater Campo de Fútbol de Vallecas stadium.

Rayo has competed in one European competition, the UEFA Cup in the 2000–01 season. The club made it to the quarter-finals before losing to fellow Spanish club Alavés 4–2 on aggregate.

name: Doncaster Rovers
bio:
Doncaster Rovers Football Club is an English football club based in Doncaster, South Yorkshire. They play in League One, the third tier of the English football league system after relegation from The Championship in 2013–14.

The club was founded in 1879 and turned professional in 1885. Doncaster have spent the majority of their playing history between the third and fourth tiers of the English football league system and are one of four clubs to win the Division 3/League Two title three times.

The club's colours have traditionally been red and white. Their home strip is red and white hoops, which has been the main design of the club's home shirt since 2001.

The associated Doncaster Rovers Belles L.F.C. are one of the most successful women's clubs in English football.

Following promotion in 2012–13, manager Brian Flynn moved to become director of football at the club, with Paul Dickov becoming manager a few weeks later.

name: FC Augsburg
bio:
Fußball-Club Augsburg 1907 e. V., commonly known as FC Augsburg (German pronunciation: ) or Augsburg, is a German football club based in Augsburg, Bavaria. FC Augsburg play in the Bundesliga, the top tier of the German football league system. The team was founded as Fußball-Klub Alemania Augsburg in 1907 and played as BC Augsburg from 1921 to 1969. With over 12,200 members, it is the largest football club in Swabian Bavaria.

FC Augsburg, which has long fluctuated between the second and third division, experienced a difficult time in the early 2000s, suffering relegation to the fourth division for two seasons. FCA recovered from this, returning to professional football in 2006. At the end of the 2010–11 season, Augsburg were promoted to the Bundesliga for the first time. Since 2009, FC Augsburg's stadium has been the WWK ARENA.

name: Real Sociedad
bio:
Anoeta Stadium, currently known as the Reale Arena for sponsorship purposes, is a football stadium in San Sebastián, Basque Country, Spain that was inaugurated in 1993.

The stadium lies at the Anoeta Sports Complex, and is mostly used for football matches where La Liga side Real Sociedad contest their home games. The total seating capacity of the stadium, which was originally 32,000, was restricted to around 26,800 due to redevelopment works; its capacity for the September 2019 completion has been quoted as 39,500 seats (down on the figure of 42,300 given two years earlier), making it the 11th-largest stadium in Spain and the 2nd-largest in the Basque Country.

name: 1. FSV Mainz 05
bio:
1. FSV Mainz 05, founded in 1905 in Mainz, Germany, is a Bundesliga club known for its passionate fanbase and distinctive carnival-influenced culture that reflects the city's vibrant traditions. The club achieved its greatest success by establishing itself as a consistent Bundesliga presence since 2009, with notable European competition appearances and a reputation for developing young talent under influential coaches like Jürgen Klopp, who led the team from 2001 to 2008. Mainz is celebrated for its intimate Mewa Arena atmosphere and strong community connections, earning respect as a model mid-sized club that punches above its weight through smart management and tactical discipline. 

Currently competing in the Bundesliga's middle tier, the club continues to focus on sustainable growth while maintaining its identity as one of German football's most authentic and well-run organizations.
extra_info: The club participates in both the Bundesliga and DFB-Pokal competitions. During the 2023-24 season, the club had three different managers, with Bo Henriksen taking over from February 13th.

name: Club Atlético de Madrid
bio:
Club Atlético de Madrid, founded in 1903, is one of Spain's most prestigious football clubs, renowned for their passionate fanbase and distinctive red-and-white striped jerseys. The club has achieved remarkable success throughout its history, winning eleven La Liga titles, ten Copa del Rey trophies, and three UEFA Europa League championships, while also reaching three UEFA Champions League finals in recent years. Atlético is celebrated for its fierce rivalry with crosstown neighbors Real Madrid and its reputation for producing tenacious, defensively solid teams that embody the working-class spirit of their supporters. 

Currently competing at the highest level of Spanish football, the club continues to be a formidable force in both domestic and European competitions under the guidance of their long-serving manager Diego Simeone.
extra_info:
The club finished 3rd in the 2024-25 Liga F season and plays their home matches at Centro Deportivo Wanda in Alcalá de Henares. They are commonly known as Atlético Madrid Women or Atleti Femenino and maintain the traditional red and white colors of the parent club.

— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Madrid —
The club has won the Primera División Femenina multiple times and regularly competes in the UEFA Women's Champions League. They are known for developing talented players and have a strong youth academy system.

— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Madrid —
Nicknamed 'Los Colchoneros' and 'Los Rojiblancos', the club moved to their current stadium in 2017. They are known for their passionate fanbase and rivalry with Real Madrid in the Madrid Derby.

name: Cagliari Calcio S.p.A.
bio:
Cagliari Calcio S.p.A., founded in 1920 and based in Sardinia's capital city, is one of Italy's most celebrated provincial clubs, having achieved legendary status by winning their only Serie A title in the 1969-70 season under the brilliance of Gigi Riva, who remains the Italian national team's all-time leading scorer. The club's greatest triumph came during that magical campaign when they became the first and only team from outside mainland Italy to claim the Scudetto, establishing themselves as a symbol of Sardinian pride and identity. 

Known for their passionate fanbase, the "Rossoblù" (Red and Blues) have cultivated a fierce loyalty among supporters who view the club as representing their island's distinct culture and resilient spirit against larger, more powerful mainland rivals. Currently competing in Serie A, Cagliari continues to be a respected fixture in Italian football, consistently punching above their weight while maintaining their role as Sardinia's premier football institution.
extra_info:
The club's greatest achievement was winning the Serie A title in 1969-70 with legendary striker Luigi Riva. Their best European performance came in the 1993-94 UEFA Cup where they reached the semi-finals before losing to Internazionale. The club finished 15th in the 2024-25 Serie A season.

— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cagliari_Calcio —
The club's colors are red and blue, and they are nicknamed 'Gli Isolani' (The Islanders). They moved to the newly built Unipol Domus stadium in 2017, having previously played at the Stadio Sant'Elia.

name: Reial Club Deportiu Espanyol de Barcelona
bio:
Reial Club Deportiu Espanyol de Barcelona, founded in 1900, is one of Spain's oldest football clubs and Barcelona's second team, traditionally representing the Spanish identity in Catalonia as a counterpoint to FC Barcelona's Catalan nationalism. The club has achieved notable success throughout its history, including four Copa del Rey victories (1929, 1940, 2000, 2006) and two UEFA Cup finals appearances (1988, 2007), with their most recent major triumph coming in the 2006 Copa del Rey under manager Ernesto Valverde. Known for their blue and white striped jerseys and nicknamed "Los Periquitos" (The Parakeets), Espanyol has cultivated a passionate fanbase despite often playing in the shadow of their city rivals, with the club's identity deeply rooted in working-class values and Spanish tradition. 

Currently competing in the Segunda División after relegation from La Liga in 2020, the club is working to return to Spain's top flight from their home at the RCDE Stadium in Cornellà-El Prat.
extra_info: Espanyol has won 4 Copa del Rey titles (1928-29, 1940, 1999-2000, 2005-06) and reached UEFA Cup finals in 1988 and 2007. The club has won the Segunda División twice and claimed 11 Campionat de Catalunya regional titles. They maintain a historic rivalry with FC Barcelona known as the Derbi barceloní and were the first Spanish club formed exclusively by Spanish supporters.

name: FC St. Pauli
bio:
Fußball-Club St Pauli von 1910 e.V., commonly known as simply FC St Pauli, is a German sports club based in the St. Pauli quarter of Hamburg.

The football department is part of a larger sports club that also has departments in rugby (FC St. Pauli Rugby), baseball, bowling, boxing (FC St. Pauli Boxen), chess, cycling, handball, roller derby (Harbour Girls Hamburg), skittles, softball and table tennis and since 2011 Marathon. Until the end of 2013, the club also had a department in American football, but it was dissolved because it lacked the youth team required in order to hold a men's team. FC St. Pauli has 22,000 members as of 1 July 2015.

The men's professional football team dropped down to the Regionalliga in 2003, at that time the third highest football division in Germany and remained there for four years. In 2007, they won promotion back to the 2. Bundesliga and in 2010, they were promoted to the Bundesliga. Since the 2011–12 season, they have played in 2. Bundesliga, the second-highest division in Germany.

FC St Pauli has a cross-town rivalry with Hamburger SV; the matches between the two are known as the Hamburger Stadtderby or simply Derby. The club also has a rivalry with Hansa Rostock. While the footballers have enjoyed only modest success on the field, the club is widely recognised for its distinctive culture and has a large popular following as one of the country's "Kult" clubs. FC St. Pauli supporters are highly identified with far-left politics.

name: Società Sportiva Calcio Napoli S.p.A.
bio: SSC Napoli, founded in 1926, is one of Italy's most storied football clubs, based in the passionate city of Naples in southern Italy. The club reached its golden era in the 1980s under the legendary Diego Maradona, winning two Serie A titles (1987, 1990) and a UEFA Cup (1989), while also claiming six Coppa Italia trophies throughout its history. Known for their distinctive sky blue jerseys and the deafening support of their devoted "tifosi" at the iconic Stadio Diego Armando Maradona, Napoli embodies the fierce pride and cultural identity of southern Italy. Under current management, the club has re-emerged as a consistent Serie A title contender and Champions League participant, challenging Italy's traditional northern powerhouses with their attacking style of play.
extra_info:
— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SSC_Napoli —
The club plays at the Stadio Diego Armando Maradona, renamed in honor of Diego Maradona in 2020. Napoli's traditional kit colors are sky blue shirts, white shorts, and sky blue socks. The club won their most recent Serie A title in the 2022-23 season, their first since 1990.

name: Athletic Club
bio: Athletic Club, commonly known as Athletic Bilbao, is a football club from Bilbao in Biscay, Basque Country, Spain. The club has played in the Primera División of La Liga since its start in 1928 and is one of only three clubs which have never been relegated from La Liga. Athletic is known for its cantera policy of bringing young Basque players through the ranks and recruiting players from other Basque clubs.
extra_info:
The team holds home matches at the 54,500-capacity San Mamés stadium or at the smaller stadium attached to the club's training facility at Lezama. As a reserve team, they serve as a pathway for young players to progress to Athletic Bilbao's first team.

— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Madrid —
Nicknamed Los Colchoneros (The Mattress Makers) and Los Rojiblancos (The Red and Whites). Known for their passionate fanbase and defensive playing style under Diego Simeone. Major rivals include Real Madrid (El Derbi Madrileño) and FC Barcelona.

— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Athletic_Bilbao —
Reserve team of Athletic Bilbao, plays in Primera División RFEF Group 2, based in Lezama training facilities

name: Preston North End
bio:
Preston North End Football Club (often shortened to PNE) is an English football club located in the Deepdale area of Preston, Lancashire. They currently play in the Championship, the second tier of the English football league system, having won promotion from League One via the play-offs at the end of the 2014–15 season.

The club was a founding member of the Football League and completed the inaugural season unbeaten to become the first league champions, in the same season winning the FA Cup without conceding a goal to become the first club to achieve the English football "Double". Preston's unbeaten League and Cup season earned them the nickname "The Invincibles".

Preston's most recent major trophy success was their FA Cup victory over Huddersfield Town F.C. in 1938. Many notable players have played for the club, including Tom Finney, Bill Shankly, Tommy Docherty, Alan Kelly, Sr. and Graham Alexander.

Based on results achieved in the Football League from 1888–89 to 2014–15, Preston were ranked as the fourth most-successful English football club of all time domestically, while only Notts County had played more Football League games than Preston.

name: Football Club Internazionale Milano S.p.A.
bio:
Football Club Internazionale Milano S.p.A., founded in 1908, is one of Italy's most prestigious football clubs, established by a group of Italian and Swiss footballers who broke away from AC Milan. The Nerazzurri have achieved remarkable success throughout their history, winning 19 Serie A titles, 3 UEFA Champions League trophies, and notably completing a historic treble in 2010 under José Mourinho. Known for their passionate fanbase and the iconic San Siro stadium they share with city rivals AC Milan, Inter embodies a culture of international inclusivity and attacking football that has attracted world-class players from across the globe. 

Currently competing in Serie A under the Suning Holdings ownership, Inter remains a formidable force in both domestic and European competitions, consistently challenging for major honors while maintaining their status as one of Italian football's "Big Three" alongside Juventus and AC Milan.
extra_info:
Inter have won 30 domestic trophies including 18 league titles, and achieved five successive league titles from 2006-2010. They have won the Champions League three times (1964, 1965, 2010), completing an unprecedented Italian continental treble in 2010. Other honours include three UEFA Cups, two Intercontinental Cups and one FIFA Club World Cup. Notable players include Giuseppe Meazza (club's record goalscorer with 245 goals) and Giuseppe Bergomi (record appearance holder with 519 games).

— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inter_Milan#Technical_staff —
Also known as Internazionale or simply Inter, the club plays at the San Siro stadium which they share with AC Milan. They are one of only three clubs to have never been relegated from Serie A.

name: Deportivo Alavés
bio:
Deportivo Alavés, S.A.D. (Spanish pronunciation: ; Sporting Alavés), usually abbreviated to Alavés, is a Spanish football club based in Vitoria-Gasteiz, Álava, in the autonomous community of the Basque Country. Founded on 1 July 1920 as Sport Friends Club, it has played in the highest football category of The Liga Nacional de Fútbol Profesional, La Liga, since the 2016–17 season. Alavés play in the Segunda División, after being relegated from La Liga in the 2021–22 season.

It is recognized as the third most successful team in the Basque Country following Athletic Club of Bilbao and Real Sociedad de Futbol of San Sebastián. Its biggest success was in 2001 when, in the year of its debut in European competition, it reached the 2001 UEFA Cup Final, where it played against Liverpool. It was defeated 5–4 by golden goal. In 2017, the club reached the final of the Copa del Rey, losing out 3–1 to Barcelona.

The team's home kit is blue and white-striped shirt, blue shorts and white socks. It holds home matches at the 19,840-seater Mendizorrotza Stadium and uses other facilities located in Ibaia dedicated to training.

name: Ipswich Town
bio:
Ipswich Town Football Club is a professional football club based in Ipswich, Suffolk, England. The club competes in the EFL Championship, the second tier of English football. 

The club was founded in 1878 but did not turn professional until 1936, and was subsequently elected to join the Football League in 1938. They play their home games at Portman Road in Ipswich. The only fully professional football club in Suffolk, they have a long-standing and fierce rivalry with Norwich City in Norfolk, with whom they have contested the East Anglian derby 139 times since 1902. The club's traditional home colours are blue shirts and white shorts.

Ipswich have won the English league title once, in their first season in the top flight in 1961–62, and have twice finished runners-up, in 1980–81 and 1981–82. They won the FA Cup in 1977–78, and the UEFA Cup in 1980–81. They have competed in the top two tiers of English football uninterrupted since 1957–58, currently the longest streak among Championship clubs after Coventry were relegated in the 2011–12 season. They have competed in all three European club competitions, and have never lost at home in European competition, defeating Real Madrid, AC Milan, Internazionale, Lazio and Barcelona, among others.of the 2014–15 season, they play in the Football League Championship, having last appeared in the Premier League in 2001–02.

The club was founded in 1878 but did not turn professional until 1936, and was subsequently elected to join the Football League in 1938. They play their home games at Portman Road in Ipswich. The only fully professional football club in Suffolk, they have a long-standing and fierce rivalry with Norwich City in Norfolk, with whom they have contested the East Anglian derby 138 times since 1902.

Ipswich won the English league title once, in their first season in the top flight in 1961–62, and have twice finished runners-up, in 1980–81 and 1981–82. They won the FA Cup in 1977–78, and the UEFA Cup in 1980–81. They have competed in the top two tiers of English football uninterrupted since 1957–58, currently the longest streak among Championship clubs after Coventry were relegated in the 2011–12 season. They have competed in all three European club competitions, and have never lost at home in European competition, defeating Real Madrid, AC Milan, Internazionale, Lazio and Barcelona, amongst others.

name: Parma Calcio 1913 S.r.l.
bio:
Parma Calcio 1913 S.r.l. is an Italian football club founded in 1913 and based in Parma, Emilia-Romagna, with a rich history that includes golden periods in both the 1990s and early 2000s. During their most successful era under president Florentino Pérez, the club won multiple UEFA Cups (1995, 1999), the European Cup Winners' Cup (1993), and several domestic trophies, establishing themselves as a formidable force in European competition. 

Known for their yellow and blue colors and passionate fanbase, Parma has cultivated a strong regional identity while maintaining a tradition of developing talented players and playing attractive football. After experiencing financial difficulties that led to bankruptcy and relegation to the lower divisions in 2015, the club has since rebuilt and currently competes in Serie A, working to restore their former glory while maintaining their distinctive footballing philosophy.
extra_info: The club is owned by the Krause Group from the USA, with additional backing from local businessmen including Guido Barilla (owner of Barilla Group) and Gian Paolo Dallara. The team trains at the Centro Sportivo di Collecchio in Collecchio and finished 16th in the 2024-25 Serie A season.

name: Borussia Mönchengladbach
bio:
Borussia VfL 1900 Mönchengladbach e.V., commonly known as Borussia Mönchengladbach, Mönchengladbach or Gladbach, is a German association football club based in Mönchengladbach, North Rhine-Westphalia. Founded in 1900, Borussia Mönchengladbach play in the Bundesliga, the top tier of the German football league system, making their first appearance in the league during the 1965–66 season. Subsequently the club became one of Germany's best-known, best-supported, and most successful teams, winning the Bundesliga five times during the 1970s.

Since 2004, Borussia Mönchengladbach have played at the 54,057 capacity Borussia-Park, having previously played at the smaller Bökelbergstadion since 1919. Borussia-Park is famous for its "Nordkurve" (or North stand), a single-tiered stand. Borussia Mönchengladbach has over 60,000 members as of September 2013 and is the fifth largest club in Germany. Their main rivals are 1. FC Köln.

"Borussia" is a Latinized form of Prussia, a popular term in naming German clubs located within the former Kingdom of Prussia. The club's nickname is Die Fohlen  (The Foals), coined in the 1970s due to having a young team with a fast, aggressive playing style. The official mascot of the club is the foal Jünter.

Borussia Mönchengladbach were formed as FC Borussia in 1900 in the Eicken district of Mönchengladbach. Borussia derives from the Latinized form of Prussia, the Kingdom in which Mönchengladbach was situated from 1815. By 1912, Die Borussen found themselves in the Verbandsliga, at the time the highest division the club could play in.

In March 1914, the club purchased De Kull, the ground on which the Bökelbergstadion would be built. The First World War halted the progress of both the stadium and FC Borussia, but by late 1917 the team had begun to play games once more. In 1919, FC Borussia merged with another local club, Turnverein Germania 1889, becoming 1899 VfTuR M.Gladbach. The club tasted its first major success in 1920, beating Kölner BC 3–1, thus winning the Westdeutsche Meisterschaft final.

The union between Germania and Borussia only lasted a matter of two years; the club was thereafter known as Borussia VfL 1900 e.V. M.Gladbach.

name: Fußball-Club Bayern München e. V.
bio: FC Bayern München, founded in 1900, is Germany's most successful football club and one of the world's most prestigious sporting institutions, having won a record 32 Bundesliga titles and six European Cup/Champions League trophies. The Bavarian giants are renowned for their attacking philosophy, world-class youth academy, and iconic players who have defined generations of German football, while maintaining a strong connection to their Munich roots and passionate fanbase. Bayern's recent dominance includes an unprecedented 11 consecutive Bundesliga titles (2013-2023) and a historic treble in 2020, cementing their status as a global powerhouse. Currently competing at the highest level domestically and internationally, the club continues to set the standard for excellence in German football while consistently challenging for major honors across all competitions.
extra_info: Bayern has won six European Cup/UEFA Champions League titles, including three consecutive titles (1974-76) and achieved continental trebles in 2013 and 2020. They became only the second team to win the sextuple in 2020. With 223,985 members, Bayern is the second-biggest sports club in the world by membership and generates significant revenue as the fourth biggest football club globally. The club has traditional rivalries with TSV 1860 München, FC Nürnberg, and contemporary rivals Borussia Dortmund.

name: TSG 1899 Hoffenheim
bio:
TSG 1899 Hoffenheim, founded in 1899 in the village of Hoffenheim, Baden-Württemberg, experienced a remarkable transformation from amateur football to the Bundesliga following significant investment from SAP co-founder Dietmar Hopp beginning in 1990. The club achieved its greatest success by reaching the UEFA Champions League group stage multiple times since their Bundesliga promotion in 2008, establishing themselves as a consistent European competitor despite their small-town origins. Known for their modern approach to football development, state-of-the-art training facilities, and commitment to youth development, Hoffenheim has cultivated a reputation as an innovative club that punches above its weight. 

Currently competing in the Bundesliga's upper half, TSG Hoffenheim continues to represent one of German football's most unique success stories, blending traditional community roots with ambitious sporting aspirations.

name: Società Sportiva Lazio S.p.A.
bio:
Società Sportiva Lazio S.p.A., founded in 1900 in Rome, is one of Italy's most historic football clubs, competing in Serie A and sharing the Stadio Olimpico with city rivals AS Roma. The club has achieved significant success throughout its history, winning two Serie A titles (1973-74, 1999-2000), seven Coppa Italia trophies, and notable European honors including the UEFA Cup Winners' Cup in 1999 and the UEFA Super Cup in 1999. 

Known for their distinctive sky blue and white colors, Lazio has cultivated a passionate fanbase called "Laziali" and maintains one of the most intense rivalries in world football with Roma in the Derby della Capitale. Currently established as a competitive force in Serie A, Lazio consistently challenges for European qualification while developing talented players through their respected youth academy system.
extra_info: Lazio have won the Coppa Italia five times, the Supercoppa Italiana three times, and both the UEFA Cup Winners' Cup and UEFA Super Cup on one occasion. Their first major success came in 1958 with the domestic cup, followed by their first Serie A title in 1974. The most successful period was in the late 1990s and early 2000s, winning the UEFA Cup Winners' Cup and UEFA Super Cup in 1999, and the Serie A title in 2000, while also reaching the UEFA Cup final in 1998.

name: Borussia Dortmund
bio:
Ballspielverein Borussia 09 e. V. Dortmund, often known simply as Borussia Dortmund or by its initialism BVB, is a German professional sports club based in Dortmund, North Rhine-Westphalia. It is best known for its men's professional football team, which plays in the Bundesliga, the top tier of the German football league system. The club have won eight league championships, five DFB-Pokals, one UEFA Champions League, one Intercontinental Cup, and one UEFA Cup Winners' Cup.

Founded in 1909 by eighteen football players from Dortmund, the football team is part of a large membership-based sports club with more than 189,000 members in 2023, making Borussia Dortmund the fifth largest sports club by membership in the world. The club also has a women's handball team. Since 1974, Dortmund have played their home games at the Westfalenstadion; the stadium is the largest in Germany, and Dortmund has the highest average attendance of any association football club in the world.

Borussia Dortmund's colours are black and yellow, giving the club its nickname die Schwarzgelben. They hold a long-standing rivalry with Ruhr neighbours Schalke 04, against whom they contest the Revierderby. They also contest Der Klassiker with Bayern Munich.

Under the directorship of Michael Zorc in the 2010s, Dortmund cultivated a reputation for spotting and developing young talent, and have remained focused on developing a youth system. As of 2024, Dortmund had the second most revenue across football clubs in Germany, and the 12th most revenue across all football teams in the world, per Deloitte's Football Money League.

name: Bolton Wanderers
bio:
Bolton Wanderers Football Club is a professional football club based in Horwich, Metropolitan Borough of Bolton, Greater Manchester, England, which competes in League One, the third level of the English football league system. 

Formed as Christ Church Football Club in 1874, it adopted its current name in 1877 and was a founder member of the Football League in 1888. Bolton have spent more seasons, 73, than any other club in the top flight without winning the title. They finished third in the First Division in 1891–92, 1920–21 and 1924–25. Bolton won the FA Cup three times in the 1920s, and again in 1958. The club spent a season in the Fourth Division in 1987–88, before regaining top-flight status in 1995 and qualifying for the UEFA Cup twice; reaching the last 32 in 2005–06 and the last 16 in 2007–08.

The club played at Burnden Park for 102 years from 1895. On 9 March 1946, thirty-three Bolton fans lost their lives in a human crush, the Burnden Park disaster. In 1997, Bolton moved to the Reebok Stadium. The stadium was renamed the Macron Stadium in 2014 and University of Bolton Stadium in 2018.

Beginning in 2015, Bolton had been in severe financial difficulties, and went into administration in May 2019. Facing possible EFL expulsion and probable extinction, the club was acquired by new owners on 28 August 2019.

name: 1. Fußballclub Union Berlin e. V.
bio:
1. Fußballclub Union Berlin e.V., founded in 1966 in East Berlin, is a German professional soccer club that has become synonymous with authentic fan culture and community spirit, famously playing at the atmospheric Stadion An der Alten Försterei. The club spent decades in lower divisions before achieving promotion to the Bundesliga in 2019, marking their first-ever appearance in Germany's top flight. Known for their passionate supporters, unique traditions like the annual Christmas carol singing event, and their motto "Eisern Union" (Iron Union), the club has cultivated one of the most dedicated fanbases in German football. 

Currently competing in the Bundesliga, Union Berlin has established themselves as a solid mid-table team while maintaining their distinctive identity as the "people's club" that stands apart from modern football commercialization.
extra_info: Union Berlin was based in East Berlin during the Cold War era and joined the common German league structure after reunification in 1990. The club is renowned for its enthusiastic and creative fan base, famous chant 'Eisern Union' (Iron Union), and their home stadium which hosts notable events like the annual Weihnachtssingen (Christmas Carols Event). Their stadium is the largest single-purpose football stadium in the German capital and has been home to the club since 1920.

name: Unione Sportiva Lecce SpA
bio:
Unione Sportiva Lecce SpA, founded in 1908 and based in the historic city of Lecce in Italy's Apulia region, is known for their distinctive yellow and red colors that have become synonymous with southern Italian football passion. The club achieved their greatest success during the early 2000s under coach Zdeněk Zeman, reaching Serie A and establishing themselves as a competitive force while developing a reputation for attractive, attacking football and nurturing young talent. 

Lecce's passionate fanbase, known as "giallorossi," creates an electric atmosphere at the Stadio Via del Mare, embodying the proud footballing culture of the Salento peninsula. After experiencing relegation battles and lower division campaigns in recent years, the club has worked to rebuild and return to Italy's top flight, continuing their tradition as an important regional institution in Italian football.
extra_info: Lecce achieved a remarkable double promotion from Serie C to Serie B and then to Serie A under previous management. The club is known as the Giallorossi due to their yellow and red colors, and represents the Salento region of southern Italy. They finished 17th in the 2024-25 Serie A season.

name: RB Leipzig
bio:
RasenBallsport Leipzig e.V., commonly known as RB Leipzig, is a German association football club based in Leipzig, Saxony. The club was founded in 2009 by initiative of the company Red Bull GmbH—which purchased the playing rights of a fifth-tier side, SSV Markranstädt, with the intent of advancing the new club to the top-flight Bundesliga within eight years. Men's professional football is run by the spin-off organization RasenballSport Leipzig GmbH. RB Leipzig plays its home matches at the Red Bull Arena.

In its inaugural season in 2009–10, RB Leipzig dominated the NOFV-Oberliga Süd (V) and was promoted as champions to the Regionalliga Nord (IV). RB Leipzig won the 2012–13 Regionalliga Nordost season without a single defeat and was promoted to the 3. Liga (III), then finished the 2013–14 3. Liga season as runners-up and was promoted to the 2. Bundesliga (II) as the first team since the introduction of the 3. Liga to win promotion after only one season.

On 8 May 2016, RB Leipzig ensured promotion to the Bundesliga for the 2016–17 season with a 2–0 win over Karlsruher SC. One year later, RB Leipzig captured a place in the 2017–18 UEFA Champions League by finishing as runners-up of the Bundesliga.

name: Rotherham United
bio:
Rotherham United Football Club, nicknamed The Millers, is a professional association football club based in Rotherham, South Yorkshire, England. The team will compete in League One, the third tier of the English football league system, in the 2021-22 season after finishing 23rd in the 2020-21 Championship.

Founded in 1925 as a merger between Rotherham County (1877) and Rotherham Town (1899), the club's colours were initially yellow and black, but changed to the red and white around 1930. Rotherham United play their home games at New York Stadium, a 12,004 capacity all-seater stadium, having previously played since its foundation at Millmoor for 101 years. Joining the Football League back in 1925, Rotherham spent the first 25 years of their time in the Third Division North, the lowest level of the Football League, finally gaining promotion to the Second Division at the end of the 1950–51 season.

The Millers featured in the inaugural League Cup final in 1961, won the 1996 Football League Trophy and the 1946 Football League North Cup. They also achieved two separate back to back promotions in 1999–2001 under Ronnie Moore and 2012–2014 under Steve Evans.

name: 1. FC Köln
bio: 1. FC Köln Frauen is 1. FC Köln's women's football section, currently competing in the Bundesliga.

name: Sport-Club Freiburg e.V.
bio:
Sport-Club Freiburg e.V., founded in 1904 in the southwestern German city of Freiburg im Breisgau, has established itself as one of the Bundesliga's most respected clubs through prudent management and community-focused values. The club achieved its greatest success by reaching the DFB-Pokal final in 2013 and consistently maintaining Bundesliga status despite operating with one of the league's smallest budgets. Known for their exceptional youth development system, sustainable approach to football, and passionate fanbase at the intimate Europa-Park Stadion, Freiburg embodies the ideal of a community club that prioritizes local talent and fiscal responsibility. 

Currently competing in the Bundesliga under head coach Christian Streich, the club continues to punch above its weight while serving as a model for sustainable football operations in modern German soccer.
extra_info: The club was promoted as champions from the 2. Bundesliga in 2016. Volker Finke, who managed the club from 1991 to 2007, was the longest-serving manager in German professional football history. Joachim Löw, current Germany national team manager, is the club's all-time leading goal scorer with 81 goals in 252 games during his three spells at the club. They finished 5th in the 2024-25 Bundesliga season.

name: Udinese Calcio 1896 SpA
bio:
Udinese Calcio 1896 SpA is a historic Italian football club founded in 1896 and based in Udine, in the northeastern Friuli-Venezia Giulia region. The club has established itself as a consistent Serie A presence, known for its exceptional youth development system and ability to discover and nurture international talent from around the world, particularly from South America and Africa. 

Under the long-standing ownership of the Pozzo family, Udinese has achieved notable success including multiple UEFA Cup participations and a strong reputation for developing players who go on to star for major European clubs and national teams. Currently competing in Serie A, the Bianconeri continue their tradition of shrewd player recruitment and development while maintaining their status as one of Italy's most internationally-minded clubs.
extra_info: The club plays at Stadio Friuli and has a large fanbase in Friuli and surrounding areas. Udinese also operates its own television channel (Udinese Channel) on digital terrestrial television in northeast Italy. The team finished 12th in the 2024-25 Serie A season.

name: Leyton Orient
bio:
Leyton Orient Football Club, commonly referred to as Orient, is a professional association football club based in Leyton, Waltham Forest, London, England. The team compete in EFL League One, the third level of the English football league system. 

Leyton Orient have spent one season in the top flight of English football, in 1962–63. In 1978, Orient reached the semi-finals of the FA Cup for the only time in their history, under the management of Jimmy Bloomfield, but were beaten 3–0 by Arsenal. Between October 1993 and September 1995, Orient did not win a single away game in the league, leaving them bottom of Division Two in 1994–95. They reached the Football League Trophy Southern Area Final in 2012–13, but were beaten 3–2 on aggregate by Southend United, missing out on a chance to go to Wembley.

Leyton Orient's home ground Brisbane Road is officially known as the Matchroom Stadium after former club chairman Barry Hearn's sports promotion company. Hearn became chairman in 1995 after the club was put on sale for £5 by then-chairman Tony Wood whose coffee-growing business in Rwanda had been destroyed in the country's civil war. The period of the club's near-closure was covered by the television documentary Orient: Club for a Fiver (made by Open Media for Channel 4). In June 2014, Hearn sold the club to Italian businessman Francesco Becchetti.

Russell Slade was appointed manager of the club in 2010 and has been successful in maintaining the club's League One status. Leyton Orient finished 7th, one place away from the playoff positions, in both the 2010/2011 and 2012/2013 seasons. He resigned on 24 September 2014.

name: Bayer 04 Leverkusen
bio:
Bayer 04 Leverkusen, founded in 1904 by employees of pharmaceutical giant Bayer AG, is a prominent German football club based in Leverkusen, North Rhine-Westphalia. Known affectionately as "Die Werkself" (The Company's Eleven), the club has established itself as a consistent force in the Bundesliga, regularly qualifying for European competitions and producing world-class talent through their renowned youth academy. Despite reaching multiple finals in domestic and international competitions, including the UEFA Champions League final in 2002, 

Leverkusen has historically been dubbed "Neverkusen" due to their tendency to finish as runners-up in major tournaments. Currently competing in the Bundesliga's top tier, the club continues to punch above their weight under their modern approach, maintaining their reputation as one of Germany's most well-run and competitive football institutions.
extra_info:
The club finished 4th in the 2024-25 Frauen-Bundesliga season. They maintain a diverse international squad with players from Germany, Austria, Denmark, Canada, England, Hungary, China, France, and Norway.

— From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bayer_04_Leverkusen —
The club is owned by Bayer AG and plays at the BayArena. They compete in European competitions regularly and have a strong youth academy. Notable former players include Michael Ballack, Bernd Schneider, and Kai Havertz.

name: Hamburger Sport-Verein e.V.
bio:
Hamburger Sport-Verein e.V. (HSV), founded in 1887, is one of Germany's most storied football clubs, historically based in Hamburg and renowned for being the only founding member of the Bundesliga to compete in every season from its inception in 1963 until their relegation in 2018. The club achieved its greatest successes during the late 1970s and early 1980s, capturing three Bundesliga titles (1979, 1982, 1983) and the European Cup in 1983, while also claiming the Cup Winners' Cup in 1977. HSV is deeply embedded in Hamburg's cultural identity, known for their passionate fanbase, the iconic Volksparkstadion, and their motto "Nur der HSV" (Only HSV), though the club has faced significant challenges in recent years. 

Currently competing in the 2. Bundesliga since 2018, HSV remains focused on returning to Germany's top flight while rebuilding both their competitive strength and financial stability.
extra_info: HSV has won the German national championship six times, the DFB-Pokal three times, and achieved European success with the 1976-77 European Cup Winners' Cup and 1982-83 European Cup victories. Their most successful period was from the mid-1970s to mid-1980s, with their last major trophy being the 1986-87 DFB-Pokal. The club is one of Germany's biggest sports clubs with over 70,000 members across all departments and has notable rivalries with Werder Bremen (Nordderby) and FC St. Pauli (Hamburg derby).
